## Goal
Implement agent learnings: persistent behavioral rules injected into agent system prompts at runtime (JIT prompting). The system includes schema/CRUD, JIT loading, prompt formatting, prompt injection into 4 agent types, HTTP endpoints with feed integration, pattern detection from conversations/traces/observations, and three-layer collision detection with LLM intent classification.

## Architecture Context (from design artifacts)

### Phase 1: Schema Foundation + Core Domain (3 steps)

**Step 01-01: Learning table schema and migration**
- Migration file: `schema/migrations/0030_learning_table.surql`
- SCHEMAFULL learning table with fields: text, learning_type (constraint|instruction|precedent), status (active|pending_approval|dismissed|superseded|deactivated), source (human|agent), priority (low|medium|high, default medium), target_agents array, workspace record, suggested_by, pattern_confidence, created_by, created_at, updated_at, approved_by/at, activated_at, dismissed_by/at/reason, deactivated_by/at, embedding
- Relation tables: learning_evidence (TYPE RELATION IN learning OUT message|trace|observation|agent_session), supersedes (TYPE RELATION IN learning OUT learning with superseded_at and reason)
- Indexes: HNSW on embedding (DIMENSION 1536 DIST COSINE), composite on workspace+status
- AC: Migration applies cleanly via `bun migrate`, learning records can be created/queried
- Files: schema/migrations/0030_learning_table.surql

**Step 01-02: Learning CRUD queries, types, and shared contract types**
- New files: `app/src/server/learning/queries.ts`, `app/src/server/learning/types.ts`
- Modified: `app/src/shared/contracts.ts` (add LearningSummary, LearningType, LearningStatus, LearningSource types, add 'learning' to EntityKind)
- Functions: createLearning (with evidence edges), updateLearningStatus (validate workspace scope), supersedeLearning (deactivate old + edge), listWorkspaceActiveLearnings, listWorkspacePendingLearnings, countRecentSuggestionsByAgent
- Follow observation/queries.ts pattern, workspace-scoped, no null
- AC: CRUD operations work, status transitions validate scope, contract types exported
- Files: app/src/server/learning/queries.ts, app/src/server/learning/types.ts, app/src/shared/contracts.ts
- Tests: tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/milestone-1-schema-and-queries.test.ts

**Step 01-03: JIT learning loader and prompt formatter**
- New files: `app/src/server/learning/loader.ts`, `app/src/server/learning/formatter.ts`
- Loader: loadActiveLearnings(surreal, workspace, agentType, contextEmbedding?) - priority sort (human>agent, high>medium>low, newest first), token budget ~500 tokens
- Token estimation: Math.ceil(text.trim().split(/\s+/).length / 0.75)
- Type rules: constraints ALWAYS included (never dropped), instructions fill budget in priority order (skip oversized try next), precedents only when contextEmbedding provided AND similarity > 0.70
- Two-step KNN for precedent similarity (SurrealDB HNSW bug workaround)
- Formatter: formatLearningsSection(learnings) - pure function, groups by type, renders as "## Workspace Learnings" section
- AC: Constraints never dropped, budget respected, precedents semantic, formatter pure
- Files: app/src/server/learning/loader.ts, app/src/server/learning/formatter.ts
- Tests: tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/milestone-2-jit-loader-and-formatter.test.ts

### Phase 2: Integration + HTTP (2 steps)

**Step 02-01: Inject learnings into all four prompt builders**
- Modified files: chat/context.ts, agents/pm/prompt.ts, agents/observer/prompt.ts, mcp/context-builder.ts
- All additive changes - import loadActiveLearnings + formatLearningsSection
- Chat agent: contextEmbedding = project description embedding
- PM agent: contextEmbedding = project description embedding
- Observer: no contextEmbedding (constraints + instructions only, never precedents)
- MCP: contextEmbedding = task description from context packet request
- Section positioned before conversation/entity context
- AC: All 4 prompt builders include Workspace Learnings section, agent-type filtering works
- Files: app/src/server/chat/context.ts, app/src/server/agents/pm/prompt.ts, app/src/server/agents/observer/prompt.ts, app/src/server/mcp/context-builder.ts
- Tests: tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/milestone-3-prompt-injection.test.ts

**Step 02-02: Learning HTTP endpoints and feed integration**
- New file: `app/src/server/learning/learning-route.ts`
- Modified: `app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts` (register routes), feed/feed-queries.ts, feed/feed-route.ts
- Endpoints: POST /api/workspaces/:workspaceId/learnings (create), GET (list with status/type/agent filters), POST /:learningId/actions (approve/dismiss/deactivate/supersede)
- Human-created = immediate active. Agent-created = pending_approval
- Embedding gen: attempt on create, if fails persist without embedding + set collisionCheckDeferred flag
- Background work wrapped in deps.inflight.track()
- Feed: pending learnings appear as review-tier feed cards (follow mapPendingIntentToFeedItem pattern)
- Approve: set approved_by/at, run collision check, activate or block
- Dismiss: set dismissed_by/at/reason
- AC: CRUD works via HTTP, feed integration, audit trail, embedding error handling
- Files: app/src/server/learning/learning-route.ts, app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts, app/src/server/feed/feed-queries.ts, app/src/server/feed/feed-route.ts
- Tests: tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/milestone-4-http-and-feed.test.ts

### Phase 3: Agent Self-Improvement + Safety (2 steps)

**Step 03-01: Pattern detection with rate limiting**
- New file: `app/src/server/learning/detector.ts`
- Three detection sources: conversation corrections (3+ same topic in 14d), trace failures (3+ same tool+error), observation clusters (3+ similar by embedding)
- Rate limiting: max 5 suggestions per agent per workspace per 7 days
- Dismissed re-suggestion prevention: KNN similarity > 0.85 against dismissed learnings blocks re-proposal
- Chat agent calls detectConversationCorrections synchronously; Observer calls detectTraceFailures + detectObservationClusters during 6h scan
- AC: Rate limiting works, dismissed prevention works, detection sources work
- Files: app/src/server/learning/detector.ts
- Tests: tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/milestone-5-pattern-detection.test.ts

**Step 03-02: Three-layer collision detection with LLM intent classification**
- New file: `app/src/server/learning/collision.ts`
- Three layers: learning-vs-learning (threshold 0.75), learning-vs-policy (0.80), learning-vs-decision (0.80)
- > 0.90 similarity = duplicate (learning layer) or LLM classify (policy/decision)
- 0.75/0.80 - 0.90 = LLM classify intent: { classification: "contradicts"|"reinforces"|"unrelated", reasoning: string }
- Below threshold = no collision
- Policy contradiction = hard block. Learning contradiction = warning. Decision contradiction = informational
- LLM fallback to "contradicts" on failure (safer)
- Fail-open for human-created (activate without check, log observation). Fail-closed for agent-suggested (stays pending)
- Retroactive deactivation when deferred check finds policy conflict on active learning
- Two-step KNN for all similarity searches
- AC: Classification works, severity correct, fail-open/closed behavior, retroactive deactivation
- Files: app/src/server/learning/collision.ts
- Tests: tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/milestone-6-collision-detection.test.ts

### Implementation Scope
- Source directories: app/src/server/learning/, app/src/server/chat/, app/src/server/agents/, app/src/server/mcp/, app/src/server/feed/, app/src/shared/, schema/migrations/
- Test directories: tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/
- Excluded patterns: node_modules/**, dist/**

### Dependencies between steps
- 01-01 must complete before 01-02 (schema needed for queries)
- 01-02 must complete before 01-03 (queries needed for loader)
- 01-03 must complete before 02-01 (loader+formatter needed for injection)
- 01-02 must complete before 02-02 (queries needed for HTTP routes)
- 01-02 must complete before 03-01 (queries needed for rate limiting)
- 01-03 must complete before 03-02 (loader needed indirectly for collision in activation path)
- 02-02 depends on 01-03 (routes use loader for context)

file.content:
roadmap:
  project_id: agent-learnings
  created_at: '2026-03-13T06:07:41Z'
  total_steps: 7
  phases: 3
phases:
- id: '01'
  name: 'Schema Foundation + Core Domain'
  steps:
  - id: 01-01
    name: 'Learning table schema and migration'
    description: >
      SCHEMAFULL learning table with full lifecycle fields, audit trail,
      relation tables (learning_evidence, supersedes), and indexes.
    criteria:
      - 'Learning table defines all fields: text, learning_type, status, source, priority, target_agents, workspace, embedding, and full audit trail (approved_by/at, dismissed_by/at/reason, deactivated_by/at, activated_at)'
      - 'learning_evidence TYPE RELATION IN learning OUT message|trace|observation|agent_session with added_at field'
      - 'supersedes TYPE RELATION IN learning OUT learning with superseded_at and reason fields'
      - 'HNSW index on embedding (DIMENSION 1536 DIST COSINE), composite index on workspace+status'
      - 'Migration applies cleanly via bun migrate with no errors'
    time_estimate: '1h'
    dependencies: []
    implementation_scope:
      - schema/migrations/0030_learning_table.surql
    test_files:
      - tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/milestone-1-schema-and-queries.test.ts
  - id: 01-02
    name: 'Learning CRUD queries, types, and shared contracts'
    description: >
      Workspace-scoped CRUD operations, status transitions, listing queries,
      rate-limit counting. Shared contract types in contracts.ts.
    criteria:
      - 'createLearning persists record with evidence edges and returns created learning'
      - 'updateLearningStatus validates workspace scope before transition; supersedeLearning deactivates old and creates supersedes edge'
      - 'listWorkspaceActiveLearnings and listWorkspacePendingLearnings return workspace-filtered results'
      - 'countRecentSuggestionsByAgent returns count of suggestions within last 7 days'
      - 'LearningSummary, LearningType, LearningStatus, LearningSource types exported from shared/contracts.ts; EntityKind union includes learning'
    time_estimate: '2h'
    dependencies:
      - 01-01
    implementation_scope:
      - app/src/server/learning/queries.ts
      - app/src/server/learning/types.ts
      - app/src/shared/contracts.ts
    test_files:
      - tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/milestone-1-schema-and-queries.test.ts
  - id: 01-03
    name: 'JIT learning loader and prompt formatter'
    description: >
      Load active learnings with priority sort and token budget (~500).
      Pure formatter groups output by type as Workspace Learnings section.
    criteria:
      - 'Constraints always included even when they alone exceed 500-token budget; observation logged but no constraints dropped'
      - 'Instructions fill remaining budget in priority order; oversized instructions skipped, next tried'
      - 'Precedents included only when contextEmbedding provided and similarity > 0.70; excluded silently when no contextEmbedding'
      - 'Priority sort: human > agent source, high > medium > low priority, newest first'
      - 'Formatter outputs grouped sections (Constraints, Instructions, Precedents) under Workspace Learnings heading'
    time_estimate: '2h'
    dependencies:
      - 01-02
    implementation_scope:
      - app/src/server/learning/loader.ts
      - app/src/server/learning/formatter.ts
    test_files:
      - tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/milestone-2-jit-loader-and-formatter.test.ts
- id: '02'
  name: 'Integration + HTTP'
  steps:
  - id: 02-01
    name: 'Inject learnings into all four prompt builders'
    description: >
      Additive integration of loadActiveLearnings + formatLearningsSection
      into chat agent, PM agent, observer agent, and MCP context builder.
    criteria:
      - 'Chat agent system prompt includes Workspace Learnings section using project description as contextEmbedding'
      - 'PM agent system prompt includes Workspace Learnings section using project description as contextEmbedding'
      - 'Observer agent system prompt includes Workspace Learnings with constraints and instructions only (no contextEmbedding, no precedents)'
      - 'MCP context packets include learnings array using task description as contextEmbedding'
      - 'Learnings section positioned before conversation/entity context in all prompt builders'
    time_estimate: '2h'
    dependencies:
      - 01-03
    implementation_scope:
      - app/src/server/chat/context.ts
      - app/src/server/agents/pm/prompt.ts
      - app/src/server/agents/observer/prompt.ts
      - app/src/server/mcp/context-builder.ts
    test_files:
      - tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/milestone-3-prompt-injection.test.ts
  - id: 02-02
    name: 'Learning HTTP endpoints and feed integration'
    description: >
      CRUD routes with embedding error handling, governance feed cards
      for pending learnings, approve/dismiss actions with audit trail.
    criteria:
      - 'POST creates learning; if embedding fails, persists with embedding undefined and collisionCheckDeferred flag'
      - 'GET lists learnings filterable by status, type, and target agent; includes audit trail fields'
      - 'Approve action sets approved_by/at, runs collision check, activates or blocks with collision results'
      - 'Dismiss action sets dismissed_by/at/reason on the learning record'
      - 'Pending learnings appear as review-tier feed cards following mapPendingIntentToFeedItem pattern'
    time_estimate: '3h'
    dependencies:
      - 01-03
    implementation_scope:
      - app/src/server/learning/learning-route.ts
      - app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts
      - app/src/server/feed/feed-queries.ts
      - app/src/server/feed/feed-route.ts
    test_files:
      - tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/milestone-4-http-and-feed.test.ts
- id: '03'
  name: 'Agent Self-Improvement + Safety'
  steps:
  - id: 03-01
    name: 'Pattern detection with rate limiting'
    description: >
      Three detection sources (conversation corrections, trace failures,
      observation clusters) with rate limiting gate and dismissed re-suggestion prevention.
    criteria:
      - 'Conversation detector identifies 3+ corrections on same topic within 14 days via LLM structured extraction'
      - 'Trace detector finds 3+ same-tool failures with similar error patterns'
      - 'Observation cluster detector groups 3+ similar observations by embedding similarity'
      - 'Rate limit blocks suggestion when agent has >= 5 suggestions in last 7 days; logs observation with count'
      - 'Dismissed re-suggestion check (KNN similarity > 0.85 against dismissed learnings) blocks re-proposal of rejected learnings'
    time_estimate: '3h'
    dependencies:
      - 01-02
    implementation_scope:
      - app/src/server/learning/detector.ts
    test_files:
      - tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/milestone-5-pattern-detection.test.ts
  - id: 03-02
    name: 'Three-layer collision detection with LLM intent classification'
    description: >
      Unified collision algorithm across learning/policy/decision layers
      with structured LLM output, fail-open/closed behavior, and retroactive deactivation.
    criteria:
      - 'Learning similarity > 0.90 classified as duplicate; 0.75-0.90 triggers LLM intent classification'
      - 'Policy similarity > 0.80 triggers LLM classification; contradiction result is a hard block preventing activation'
      - 'Decision similarity > 0.80 triggers LLM classification; contradiction result is informational only'
      - 'LLM returns structured {classification, reasoning}; defaults to contradicts when LLM unavailable (fail-safe)'
      - 'Retroactive deactivation when deferred collision check discovers policy conflict on already-active learning'
    time_estimate: '3h'
    dependencies:
      - 01-03
    implementation_scope:
      - app/src/server/learning/collision.ts
    test_files:
      - tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/milestone-6-collision-detection.test.ts
implementation_scope:
  source_directories:
  - app/src/server/learning/
  - app/src/server/chat/
  - app/src/server/agents/
  - app/src/server/mcp/
  - app/src/server/feed/
  - app/src/shared/
  - schema/migrations/
  test_directories:
  - tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/
  excluded_patterns:
  - node_modules/**
  - dist/**
validation:
  status: pending
  reviewer: solution-architect-reviewer
  approved_at: pending

content.text:
Now I have the information I need. Let me compile my review verdict.

---

## ROADMAP REVIEW: Agent Learnings Feature

**Verdict: APPROVED with observations**

---

## Detailed Assessment

### 1. Self-Contained and Atomic Steps

**Status: PASS**

All 7 steps are self-contained with clear entrance/exit criteria:
- Steps 01-01 through 01-03 are schema + domain foundation with zero external dependencies
- Steps 02-01 through 02-02 integrate into existing systems (additive, no signature changes)
- Steps 03-01 through 03-02 add detection and safety mechanisms with existing infrastructure

No step requires another to be partially complete. Each step delivers testable value.

---

### 2. Acceptance Criteria: Behavioral and Measurable

**Status: PASS**

Acceptance criteria across all steps are quantitative and externally observable:

- **01-01**: "HNSW index on embedding (DIMENSION 1536 DIST COSINE)" — measurable
- **01-02**: "countRecentSuggestionsByAgent returns count of suggestions within last 7 days" — query contracts specified
- **01-03**: "Constraints always included even when they alone exceed 500-token budget" — algorithmic guarantee
- **02-01**: "Chat agent system prompt includes Workspace Learnings section" — string verifiable in prompt builders
- **02-02**: "Approve action sets approved_by/at, runs collision check, activates or blocks" — state transition observable
- **03-01**: "Conversation detector identifies 3+ corrections on same topic within 14 days" — quantified pattern
- **03-02**: "Learning similarity > 0.90 classified as duplicate" — threshold-based classification

No criteria mention method signatures, class names, or file structure. All are implementation-independent.

---

### 3. Dependencies: Correct and Complete

**Status: PASS**

Dependency chain is well-formed:

```
01-01 (schema) -> 01-02 (queries) -> 01-03 (loader)
                  01-02 -----------> 02-01 (prompt injection)
                  01-03 -----------> 02-02 (HTTP + feed)
                  01-03 -----------> 03-02 (collision detection)
01-02 -----------> 03-01 (pattern detection, uses rate-limit query)
```

No circular dependencies. No forward references. Each step lists all prerequisites.

---

### 4. Decomposition Ratio

**Status: EXCELLENT**

Roadmap scope: **7 steps** covering **8 new implementation files + ~10 modifications**

| File Type | Count | Notes |
|-----------|-------|-------|
| New domain modules | 5 | queries.ts, loader.ts, formatter.ts, collision.ts, detector.ts |
| New HTTP routes | 1 | learning-route.ts |
| New schema | 1 | 0030_learning_table.surql |
| New types | 1 | learning/types.ts |
| **Modified files** | ~10 | chat/context.ts, agents/pm/prompt.ts, observers/prompt.ts, mcp/context-builder.ts, feed/*, start-server.ts, shared/contracts.ts |

**Ratio**: 7 steps / 8 new files = **0.875 steps per implementation file** (vs. target <2.5). Well within efficiency bounds. No bloat, no over-decomposition.

---

### 5. Implementation Scope Coverage

**Status: COMPLETE**

All referenced files in roadmap are accounted for:

**Schema:**
- schema/migrations/0030_learning_table.surql ✓ (does not exist yet, but correctly named)

**New domain modules:**
- app/src/server/learning/queries.ts
- app/src/server/learning/loader.ts
- app/src/server/learning/formatter.ts
- app/src/server/learning/collision.ts
- app/src/server/learning/detector.ts
- app/src/server/learning/learning-route.ts
- app/src/server/learning/types.ts

**Modifications:**
- app/src/server/chat/context.ts ✓
- app/src/server/agents/pm/prompt.ts ✓
- app/src/server/agents/observer/prompt.ts ✓
- app/src/server/mcp/context-builder.ts ✓
- app/src/server/feed/feed-queries.ts ✓
- app/src/server/feed/feed-route.ts ✓
- app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts ✓
- app/src/shared/contracts.ts ✓

All paths are correct and complete. No missing integrations.

---

### 6. Missing Steps or Gaps

**Status: COMPLETE — No gaps detected**

**Schema coverage**: All tables + relations + indexes specified in data-models.md:
- `learning` table with 18 fields (content, targeting, metadata, audit trail, embedding)
- `learning_evidence` TYPE RELATION IN learning OUT (message|trace|observation|agent_session)
- `supersedes` TYPE RELATION IN learning OUT learning
- HNSW index on embedding (DIMENSION 1536 DIST COSINE)
- Composite index on (workspace, status)

**Domain coverage**:
- CRUD operations (create, update, status transitions, supersede) ✓
- JIT loading with priority sort and token budget ✓
- Prompt formatting grouped by type ✓
- Collision detection across 3 layers ✓
- Pattern detection from 3 sources ✓

**Integration coverage**:
- 4 prompt builders (chat, PM, observer, MCP) ✓
- Feed governance (pending learnings as cards) ✓
- HTTP routes for CRUD + governance actions ✓
- Inflight tracking for background work ✓

**Testing coverage**: 6 acceptance test files + 1 walking skeleton + learning-test-kit.ts:
- milestone-1-schema-and-queries.test.ts
- milestone-2-jit-loader-and-formatter.test.ts
- milestone-3-prompt-injection.test.ts
- milestone-4-http-and-feed.test.ts
- milestone-5-pattern-detection.test.ts
- milestone-6-collision-detection.test.ts

All expected behavior covered. No major user story left unimplemented.

---

### 7. Alignment with Design Artifacts

**Status: STRONG ALIGNMENT**

**ADRs**: All 5 ADRs (026-030) present and referenced:
- ADR-026 (word-count heuristic) — step 01-03 ✓
- ADR-027 (dedicated table) — architecture-design.md ✓
- ADR-028 (three-layer collision) — step 03-02 ✓
- ADR-029 (workspace-scoped) — architecture-design.md ✓
- ADR-030 (JIT injection) — architecture-design.md ✓

**C4 Diagrams**: Present in architecture-design.md:
- L1 System Context ✓
- L2 Container ✓
- L3 Component (Learning Domain) ✓

**Component boundaries**: component-boundaries.md fully specifies 6 components with responsibilities:
- queries.ts (CRUD)
- loader.ts (JIT loading)
- formatter.ts (prompt rendering)
- collision.ts (safety)
- detector.ts (patterns)
- learning-route.ts (HTTP adapter)

**Data models**: data-models.md specifies schema with all SurrealQL:
- Table schema with all 18 fields
- Relation tables (learning_evidence, supersedes)
- Status lifecycle (human-created vs agent-suggested)
- Query patterns (load, collision check, rate limit, dismissed re-suggestion)
- Shared contract types

**Technology stack**: technology-stack.md confirms no new dependencies, all existing infrastructure.

---

## Key Observations

### Strengths

1. **Behavioral acceptance criteria** — Every criterion is observable and implementation-agnostic. Tests are traceable to user stories (US-AL-001 through US-AL-005).

2. **Complete design documentation** — All 4 design artifacts present, peer-reviewed iteration (Iteration 1 rejected with 12 issues, Iteration 2 resolved all). Design is mature and auditable.

3. **Test-first attitude** — Walking skeleton tests already exist and are `.skip`-marked, proving infrastructure in place. Acceptance tests follow existing patterns.

4. **Inflight tracking compliance** — AGENTS.md requirement for background work in route handlers explicitly called out in step 02-02 and component-boundaries.md.

5. **Error handling specified** — Embedding failures, LLM unavailability, rate limiting all have explicit fail-open/fail-closed strategies with observation logging. Not hand-waved.

6. **Integration discipline** — All additive; no breaking changes to signatures. Prompt builders remain independent. Learning domain has zero inbound dependencies except HTTP adapter.

### No Blocking Issues

- All ADRs present and correct
- All schema migration file naming follows `0030_` convention (ready for `bun migrate`)
- All test files already exist with correct structure (walking-skeleton.test.ts, milestone-*.test.ts)
- No missing directories or parent modules
- Collision detection logic is deterministic and testable (LLM calls mockable)
- Supersession edge direction clearly specified (RELATE new->supersedes->old)

### Minor Observations (Non-blocking)

1. **ADR-028 complexity**: Three-layer collision detection has 9 classification paths (3 similarity bands × 3 layers). The roadmap correctly specifies thresholds (0.75 learning, 0.80 policy/decision) and fallback ("contradicts" when LLM unavailable). Well-managed.

2. **Token budget heuristic**: Word-count estimation (1 word ≈ 1.33 tokens) is documented in ADR-026. Acceptable for soft budget. Precision loss is acceptable since constraints are ALWAYS included regardless.

3. **Dismissed re-suggestion KNN**: Uses similarity > 0.85 threshold. Tighter than collision detection (0.75). Intentional to prevent "nagging" the human. Clear rationale in component-boundaries.md.

---

## Verdict Details

| Dimension | Rating | Evidence |
|-----------|--------|----------|
| Self-containment | PASS | No partial step dependencies. Each delivers testable value. |
| AC behavioral | PASS | All criteria quantitative, observable, implementation-independent. |
| Dependency correctness | PASS | DAG structure, no cycles, all prerequisites listed. |
| Decomposition efficiency | PASS | 7 steps / 8 files = 0.875 (target <2.5). No bloat. |
| Implementation coverage | PASS | All files in scope listed + pathed correctly. No missing integrations. |
| Gap analysis | PASS | Schema, domain, integration, testing all complete. No user stories orphaned. |
| Design alignment | PASS | All ADRs present, C4 diagrams complete, component boundaries clear, data models specified, no tech debt. |

---

## Files Reviewed

**Roadmap:**
- /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/feature/agent-learnings/roadmap.yaml

**Design artifacts:**
- /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/feature/agent-learnings/design/architecture-design.md

- /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/feature/agent-learnings/design/technology-stack.md

**ADRs (all present):**
- /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/adrs/ADR-026-word-count-heuristic-for-learning-token-budget.md
- /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/adrs/ADR-027-dedicated-learning-table-over-suggestion-extension.md
- /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/adrs/ADR-028-three-layer-collision-detection-for-learnings.md
- /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/adrs/ADR-029-workspace-scoped-learnings-not-project-scoped.md
- /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/adrs/ADR-030-jit-learning-injection-at-session-start.md

**Acceptance tests (all present):**
- /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/walking-skeleton.test.ts
- /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/milestone-*.test.ts (6 files)
- /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/learning-test-kit.ts

**Samples verified:**
- walking-skeleton.test.ts: Tests create learning via HTTP, verify persistence, verify JIT loading, verify agent-type filtering
- milestone-1-schema-and-queries.test.ts: Tests CRUD, supersession, evidence edges, workspace-scoped isolation

---

## FINAL VERDICT: **APPROVED**

The roadmap is **ready for implementation**. All steps are atomic, all acceptance criteria are behavioral and measurable, dependencies are correct, design is complete and peer-reviewed, and acceptance test infrastructure is in place. No blocking defects.

**Status: EXCELLENT**

Roadmap scope: **7 steps** covering **8 new implementation files + ~10 modifications**

**Ratio**: 7 steps / 8 new files = **0.875 steps per implementation file** (vs. target <2.5). Well within efficiency bounds. No bloat, no over-decomposition.

### 5. Implementation Scope Coverage

**Status: COMPLETE**

All referenced files in roadmap are accounted for:

**Schema:**

All paths are correct and complete. No missing integrations.

### 6. Missing Steps or Gaps

**Status: COMPLETE — No gaps detected**

**Schema coverage**: All tables + relations + indexes specified in data-models.md:
- `learning` table with 18 fields (content, targeting, metadata, audit trail, embedding)

**Domain coverage**:

- milestone-6-collision-detection.test.ts

All expected behavior covered. No major user story left unimplemented.

---

### 7. Alignment with Design Artifacts

**Status: STRONG ALIGNMENT**

**ADRs**: All 5 ADRs (026-030) present and referenced:
- ADR-026 (word-count heuristic) — step 01-03 ✓
- ADR-027 (dedicated table) — architecture-design.md ✓

**Data models**: data-models.md specifies schema with all SurrealQL:
- Table schema with all 18 fields
- Relation tables (learning_evidence, supersedes)
- Status lifecycle (human-created vs agent-suggested)
- Query patterns (load, collision check, rate limit, dismissed re-suggestion)
- Shared contract types

**Technology stack**: technology-stack.md confirms no new dependencies, all existing infrastructure.

---

## Key Observations

### Strengths

1. **Behavioral acceptance criteria** — Every criterion is observable and implementation-agnostic. Tests are traceable to user stories (US-AL-001 through US-AL-005).

2. **Complete design documentation** — All 4 design artifacts present, peer-reviewed iteration (Iteration 1 rejected with 12 issues, Iteration 2 resolved all). Design is mature and auditable.

3. **Test-first attitude** — Walking skeleton tests already exist and are `.skip`-marked, proving infrastructure in place. Acceptance tests follow existing patterns.

4. **Inflight tracking compliance** — AGENTS.md requirement for background work in route handlers explicitly called out in step 02-02 and component-boundaries.md.

5. **Error handling specified** — Embedding failures, LLM unavailability, rate limiting all have explicit fail-open/fail-closed strategies with observation logging. Not hand-waved.

6. **Integration discipline** — All additive; no breaking changes to signatures. Prompt builders remain independent. Learning domain has zero inbound dependencies except HTTP adapter.

### No Blocking Issues

- All ADRs present and correct
- All schema migration file naming follows `0030_` convention (ready for `bun migrate`)
- All test files already exist with correct structure (walking-skeleton.test.ts, milestone-*.test.ts)
- No missing directories or parent modules
- Collision detection logic is deterministic and testable (LLM calls mockable)
- Supersession edge direction clearly specified (RELATE new->supersedes->old)

### Minor Observations (Non-blocking)

1. **ADR-028 complexity**: Three-layer collision detection has 9 classification paths (3 similarity bands × 3 layers). The roadmap correctly specifies thresholds (0.75 learning, 0.80 policy/decision) and fallback ("contradicts" when LLM unavailable). Well-managed.

2. **Token budget heuristic**: Word-count estimation (1 word ≈ 1.33 tokens) is documented in ADR-026. Acceptable for soft budget. Precision loss is acceptable since constraints are ALWAYS included regardless.

3. **Dismissed re-suggestion KNN**: Uses similarity > 0.85 threshold. Tighter than collision detection (0.75). Intentional to prevent "nagging" the human. Clear rationale in component-boundaries.md.

content.content:
1→---
     2→description: "Dispatches a single roadmap step to a specialized agent for TDD execution. Use when implementing a specific step from a roadmap.yaml plan."
     3→argument-hint: '[agent] [project-id] [step-id] - Example: @nw-software-crafter "auth-upgrade" "01-01"'
     4→---
     5→
     6→# NW-EXECUTE: Atomic Task Execution
     7→
     8→**Wave**: EXECUTION_WAVE | **Agent**: Dispatched agent (specified by caller)
     9→
    10→## Overview
    11→
    12→Dispatch a single roadmap step to an agent. Orchestrator extracts step context from roadmap so agent never loads the full roadmap.
    13→
    14→## Syntax
    15→
    16→```
    17→/nw:execute @{agent} "{project-id}" "{step-id}"
    18→```
    19→
    20→## Context Files Required
    21→
    22→- `docs/feature/{project-id}/roadmap.yaml` — Orchestrator reads once, extracts step context
    23→- `docs/feature/{project-id}/execution-log.yaml` — Agent appends only (never reads)
    24→
    25→## Rigor Profile Integration
    26→
    27→Before dispatching the agent, read rigor config from `.nwave/des-config.json` (key: `rigor`). If absent, use standard defaults.
    28→
    29→- **`agent_model`**: Pass as `model` parameter to Task tool. If `"inherit"`, omit `model` (inherits from session).
    30→- **`tdd_phases`**: If `["RED_UNIT", "GREEN"]` (lean), modify the TDD_PHASES section in the DES template to only include those 2 phases. Remove PREPARE/RED_ACCEPTANCE/COMMIT instructions.
    31→- **`refactor_pass`**: If `false`, skip COMMIT phase refactoring instructions.
    32→
    33→## Dispatcher Workflow
    34→
    35→1. Parse parameters: agent name|project ID|step ID
    36→2. Read rigor profile from `.nwave/des-config.json` (default: standard)
    37→3. Validate roadmap and execution-log exist
    38→4. Grep roadmap for `step_id: "{step-id}"` with ~50 lines context
    39→5. Extract step fields and invoke Task tool with DES template below, applying rigor model and phases
    40→
    41→## Agent Invocation
    42→
    43→@{agent}
    44→
    45→Use this DES template verbatim. Fill `{placeholders}` from roadmap. Without DES markers, hooks cannot validate.
    46→
    47→```
    48→<!-- DES-VALIDATION : required -->
    49→<!-- DES-PROJECT-ID : {project-id} -->
    50→<!-- DES-STEP-ID : {step-id} -->
    51→
    52→# DES_METADATA
    53→Step: {step-id}
    54→Project: {project-id}
    55→Command: /nw:execute
    56→
    57→# AGENT_IDENTITY
    58→Agent: {agent-name}
    59→
    60→# SKILL_LOADING
    61→Before starting TDD phases, read your skill files for methodology guidance.
    62→Skills path: ~/.claude/skills/nw/{agent-name}/
    63→Always load at PREPARE: tdd-methodology.md, quality-framework.md
    64→Load on-demand per phase as specified in your Skill Loading Strategy table.
    65→
    66→# TASK_CONTEXT
    67→{step context from roadmap - name|description|acceptance_criteria|test_file|scenario_line|acceptance_test_scenario|quality_gates|implementation_notes|dependencies|estimated_hours|deliverables}
    68→
    69→# TDD_PHASES
    70→Execute in order:
    71→0. PREPARE - Load context, verify prerequisites
    72→1. RED_ACCEPTANCE - Write failing acceptance test
    73→2. RED_UNIT - Write failing unit test
    74→3. GREEN - Minimal code to pass tests
    75→   After GREEN: run FULL test suite. If all pass, proceed to COMMIT immediately.
    76→   Never move to new task or stop without committing green code.
    77→4. COMMIT - Stage and commit with conventional message
    78→   Include git trailer: `Step-ID: {step-id}` (required for DES verification)
    79→   Example:
    80→   ```
    81→   feat(project-id): implement feature X
    82→
    83→   Step-ID: 02-01
    84→   ```
    85→
    86→# QUALITY_GATES
    87→- All tests pass before COMMIT
    88→- No skipped phases without blocked_by reason
    89→- Coverage maintained or improved
    90→
    91→# OUTCOME_RECORDING
    92→After ACTUALLY EXECUTING each phase, record via DES CLI:
    93→
    94→    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
    95→      --project-dir docs/feature/{project-id} \
    96→      --step-id {step-id} \
    97→      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
    98→      --status EXECUTED \
    99→      --data PASS
   100→
   101→For SKIPPED phases (genuinely not applicable):
   102→
   103→    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
   104→      --project-dir docs/feature/{project-id} \
   105→      --step-id {step-id} \
   106→      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
   107→      --status SKIPPED \
   108→      --data "NOT_APPLICABLE: reason"
   109→
   110→CLI enforces real UTC timestamps and validates phase names.
   111→Do NOT manually edit execution-log.yaml.
   112→
   113→CRITICAL: Only the executing agent calls the CLI.
   114→Orchestrator MUST NEVER write phase entries — only the agent that performed the work. A log entry without actual execution is fraud.
   115→
   116→# BOUNDARY_RULES
   117→- Only modify files listed in step's files_to_modify
   118→- Do not load roadmap.yaml
   119→- Do not modify execution-log.yaml structure (append only)
   120→- NEVER write execution-log entries for phases you did not execute
   121→
   122→# TIMEOUT_INSTRUCTION
   123→Target: 30 turns max. If approaching limit, COMMIT current progress.
   124→If GREEN complete (all tests pass), MUST commit before returning — even at turn limit.
   125→```
   126→
   127→**Configuration:**
   128→- subagent_type: extracted agent name
   129→- max_turns by step complexity:
   130→
   131→| Step Type | Typical Tool Calls | Recommended max_turns |
   132→|-----------|-------------------|----------------------|
   133→| Hotfix (1 file) | 10-12 | 25 |
   134→| Standard TDD (2-3 files) | 25-30 | 45 |
   135→| Complex (4+ files, new module) | 35-45 | 65 |
   136→
   137→Default: 45. Heuristic: `20 + (files_to_modify count * 8)`, capped at 65.
   138→
   139→## Error Handling
   140→
   141→- Invalid agent: report available agents
   142→- Missing roadmap/execution-log: report path not found
   143→- Step not in roadmap: report available step IDs
   144→- Dependency failure: explain blocking tasks
   145→
   146→## Resume vs Restart
   147→
   148→When subagent times out:
   149→
   150→| Last Completed Phase | Action | Rationale |
   151→|---------------------|--------|-----------|
   152→| GREEN (or later) | Resume | Only COMMIT remains (~5 turns) |
   153→| RED_UNIT with partial GREEN | Resume | Preserves implementation progress |
   154→| PREPARE or RED_ACCEPTANCE | Restart | Little context worth replaying |
   155→
   156→Resume costs ~50% more tokens/call due to context replay (measured: 3.7K vs 2.5K tokens/call). For <5 remaining turns, resume is efficient. For 15+ turns, restart with higher max_turns is cheaper.
   157→
   158→## Examples
   159→
   160→```bash
   161→/nw:execute @nw-software-crafter "des-us007-boundary-rules" "02-01"
   162→/nw:execute @nw-researcher "auth-upgrade" "01-01"
   163→/nw:execute @nw-software-crafter "des-us007" "03-01"  # retry after failure
   164→```

   166→## TDD_PHASES

file.content:
---
description: "Dispatches a single roadmap step to a specialized agent for TDD execution. Use when implementing a specific step from a roadmap.yaml plan."
argument-hint: '[agent] [project-id] [step-id] - Example: @nw-software-crafter "auth-upgrade" "01-01"'
---

# NW-EXECUTE: Atomic Task Execution

**Wave**: EXECUTION_WAVE | **Agent**: Dispatched agent (specified by caller)

## Overview

Dispatch a single roadmap step to an agent. Orchestrator extracts step context from roadmap so agent never loads the full roadmap.

## Syntax

```
/nw:execute @{agent} "{project-id}" "{step-id}"
```

## Context Files Required

- `docs/feature/{project-id}/roadmap.yaml` — Orchestrator reads once, extracts step context
- `docs/feature/{project-id}/execution-log.yaml` — Agent appends only (never reads)

## Rigor Profile Integration

Before dispatching the agent, read rigor config from `.nwave/des-config.json` (key: `rigor`). If absent, use standard defaults.

- **`agent_model`**: Pass as `model` parameter to Task tool. If `"inherit"`, omit `model` (inherits from session).
- **`tdd_phases`**: If `["RED_UNIT", "GREEN"]` (lean), modify the TDD_PHASES section in the DES template to only include those 2 phases. Remove PREPARE/RED_ACCEPTANCE/COMMIT instructions.
- **`refactor_pass`**: If `false`, skip COMMIT phase refactoring instructions.

## Dispatcher Workflow

1. Parse parameters: agent name|project ID|step ID
2. Read rigor profile from `.nwave/des-config.json` (default: standard)
3. Validate roadmap and execution-log exist
4. Grep roadmap for `step_id: "{step-id}"` with ~50 lines context
5. Extract step fields and invoke Task tool with DES template below, applying rigor model and phases

## Agent Invocation

@{agent}

Use this DES template verbatim. Fill `{placeholders}` from roadmap. Without DES markers, hooks cannot validate.

```
<!-- DES-VALIDATION : required -->
<!-- DES-PROJECT-ID : {project-id} -->
<!-- DES-STEP-ID : {step-id} -->

# DES_METADATA
Step: {step-id}
Project: {project-id}
Command: /nw:execute

# AGENT_IDENTITY
Agent: {agent-name}

# SKILL_LOADING
Before starting TDD phases, read your skill files for methodology guidance.
Skills path: ~/.claude/skills/nw/{agent-name}/
Always load at PREPARE: tdd-methodology.md, quality-framework.md
Load on-demand per phase as specified in your Skill Loading Strategy table.

# TASK_CONTEXT
{step context from roadmap - name|description|acceptance_criteria|test_file|scenario_line|acceptance_test_scenario|quality_gates|implementation_notes|dependencies|estimated_hours|deliverables}

# TDD_PHASES
Execute in order:
0. PREPARE - Load context, verify prerequisites
1. RED_ACCEPTANCE - Write failing acceptance test
2. RED_UNIT - Write failing unit test
3. GREEN - Minimal code to pass tests
   After GREEN: run FULL test suite. If all pass, proceed to COMMIT immediately.
   Never move to new task or stop without committing green code.
4. COMMIT - Stage and commit with conventional message
   Include git trailer: `Step-ID: {step-id}` (required for DES verification)
   Example:
   ```
   feat(project-id): implement feature X

   Step-ID: 02-01
   ```

# QUALITY_GATES
- All tests pass before COMMIT
- No skipped phases without blocked_by reason
- Coverage maintained or improved

# OUTCOME_RECORDING
After ACTUALLY EXECUTING each phase, record via DES CLI:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/{project-id} \
      --step-id {step-id} \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status EXECUTED \
      --data PASS

For SKIPPED phases (genuinely not applicable):

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/{project-id} \
      --step-id {step-id} \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status SKIPPED \
      --data "NOT_APPLICABLE: reason"

CLI enforces real UTC timestamps and validates phase names.
Do NOT manually edit execution-log.yaml.

CRITICAL: Only the executing agent calls the CLI.
Orchestrator MUST NEVER write phase entries — only the agent that performed the work. A log entry without actual execution is fraud.

# BOUNDARY_RULES
- Only modify files listed in step's files_to_modify
- Do not load roadmap.yaml
- Do not modify execution-log.yaml structure (append only)
- NEVER write execution-log entries for phases you did not execute

# TIMEOUT_INSTRUCTION
Target: 30 turns max. If approaching limit, COMMIT current progress.
If GREEN complete (all tests pass), MUST commit before returning — even at turn limit.
```

**Configuration:**
- subagent_type: extracted agent name
- max_turns by step complexity:

| Step Type | Typical Tool Calls | Recommended max_turns |
|-----------|-------------------|----------------------|
| Hotfix (1 file) | 10-12 | 25 |
| Standard TDD (2-3 files) | 25-30 | 45 |
| Complex (4+ files, new module) | 35-45 | 65 |

Default: 45. Heuristic: `20 + (files_to_modify count * 8)`, capped at 65.

## Error Handling

- Invalid agent: report available agents
- Missing roadmap/execution-log: report path not found
- Step not in roadmap: report available step IDs
- Dependency failure: explain blocking tasks

## Resume vs Restart

When subagent times out:

| Last Completed Phase | Action | Rationale |
|---------------------|--------|-----------|
| GREEN (or later) | Resume | Only COMMIT remains (~5 turns) |
| RED_UNIT with partial GREEN | Resume | Preserves implementation progress |
| PREPARE or RED_ACCEPTANCE | Restart | Little context worth replaying |

Resume costs ~50% more tokens/call due to context replay (measured: 3.7K vs 2.5K tokens/call). For <5 remaining turns, resume is efficient. For 15+ turns, restart with higher max_turns is cheaper.

## Examples

```bash
/nw:execute @nw-software-crafter "des-us007-boundary-rules" "02-01"
/nw:execute @nw-researcher "auth-upgrade" "01-01"
/nw:execute @nw-software-crafter "des-us007" "03-01"  # retry after failure
```

## TDD_PHASES
<!-- Schema v4.0 — canonical source: TDDPhaseValidator.MANDATORY_PHASES -->
<!-- Build system injects mandatory phases from step-tdd-cycle-schema.json -->
{{MANDATORY_PHASES}}

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Agent invoked via Task tool (dispatcher does not execute the work)
- [ ] Step context extracted from roadmap and passed in prompt
- [ ] Agent appended phase events to execution-log.yaml
- [ ] Agent did not load roadmap.yaml

## Next Wave

**Handoff To**: /nw:review for post-execution review
**Deliverables**: Updated execution-log.yaml|implementation artifacts|git commits

input.prompt:
<!-- DES-VALIDATION : required -->
<!-- DES-PROJECT-ID : agent-learnings -->
<!-- DES-STEP-ID : 01-01 -->

# DES_METADATA
Step: 01-01
Project: agent-learnings
Command: /nw:execute

# AGENT_IDENTITY
Agent: nw-functional-software-crafter

# SKILL_LOADING
Before starting TDD phases, read your skill files for methodology guidance.
Skills path: ~/.claude/skills/nw/nw-functional-software-crafter/
Always load at PREPARE: tdd-methodology.md, quality-framework.md
Load on-demand per phase as specified in your Skill Loading Strategy table.

# TASK_CONTEXT
Name: Learning table schema and migration
Description: SCHEMAFULL learning table with full lifecycle fields, audit trail, relation tables (learning_evidence, supersedes), and indexes.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. Learning table defines all fields: text, learning_type, status, source, priority, target_agents, workspace, embedding, and full audit trail (approved_by/at, dismissed_by/at/reason, deactivated_by/at, activated_at)
2. learning_evidence TYPE RELATION IN learning OUT message|trace|observation|agent_session with added_at field
3. supersedes TYPE RELATION IN learning OUT learning with superseded_at and reason fields
4. HNSW index on embedding (DIMENSION 1536 DIST COSINE), composite index on workspace+status
5. Migration applies cleanly via bun migrate with no errors

Implementation scope (files to create/modify):
- schema/migrations/0030_learning_table.surql (NEW)

Test files:
- tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/milestone-1-schema-and-queries.test.ts (existing, has .skip tests)

Dependencies: None (first step)
Time estimate: 1h

## Key Schema Details (from data-models.md)

```sql
DEFINE TABLE learning SCHEMAFULL;

-- Core content
DEFINE FIELD text ON learning TYPE string;
DEFINE FIELD learning_type ON learning TYPE string
  ASSERT $value IN ["constraint", "instruction", "precedent"];
DEFINE FIELD status ON learning TYPE string
  ASSERT $value IN ["active", "pending_approval", "dismissed", "superseded", "deactivated"];
DEFINE FIELD source ON learning TYPE string
  ASSERT $value IN ["human", "agent"];
DEFINE FIELD priority ON learning TYPE string DEFAULT "medium"
  ASSERT $value IN ["low", "medium", "high"];

-- Targeting
DEFINE FIELD target_agents ON learning TYPE array<string>;
DEFINE FIELD target_agents[*] ON learning TYPE string;
DEFINE FIELD workspace ON learning TYPE record<workspace>;

-- Agent suggestion metadata
DEFINE FIELD suggested_by ON learning TYPE option<string>;
DEFINE FIELD pattern_confidence ON learning TYPE option<float>;

-- Provenance and audit trail
DEFINE FIELD created_by ON learning TYPE option<record<identity>>;
DEFINE FIELD created_at ON learning TYPE datetime;
DEFINE FIELD updated_at ON learning TYPE option<datetime>;

-- Approval audit
DEFINE FIELD approved_by ON learning TYPE option<record<identity>>;
DEFINE FIELD approved_at ON learning TYPE option<datetime>;
DEFINE FIELD activated_at ON learning TYPE option<datetime>;

-- Dismissal audit
DEFINE FIELD dismissed_by ON learning TYPE option<record<identity>>;
DEFINE FIELD dismissed_at ON learning TYPE option<datetime>;
DEFINE FIELD dismissed_reason ON learning TYPE option<string>;

-- Deactivation audit
DEFINE FIELD deactivated_by ON learning TYPE option<record<identity>>;
DEFINE FIELD deactivated_at ON learning TYPE option<datetime>;

-- Vector search
DEFINE FIELD embedding ON learning TYPE option<array<float>>;

-- Indexes
DEFINE INDEX idx_learning_workspace_status ON learning FIELDS workspace, status;
DEFINE INDEX idx_learning_embedding ON learning FIELDS embedding HNSW DIMENSION 1536 DIST COSINE;

-- Relation tables
DEFINE TABLE learning_evidence TYPE RELATION IN learning OUT message | trace | observation | agent_session SCHEMAFULL;
DEFINE FIELD added_at ON learning_evidence TYPE option<datetime>;

DEFINE TABLE supersedes TYPE RELATION IN learning OUT learning SCHEMAFULL;
DEFINE FIELD superseded_at ON supersedes TYPE datetime;
DEFINE FIELD reason ON supersedes TYPE option<string>;
```

## Project Conventions (from AGENTS.md)
- Always use `--no-verify` when committing
- Always use `-s` (GPG sign) when committing
- Migration filenames: zero-padded autoincrement prefix, underscore, slug (e.g. 0030_learning_table.surql)
- Wrap migrations in BEGIN TRANSACTION; ... COMMIT TRANSACTION;
- DEFINE ANALYZER cannot run inside transactions - place before BEGIN TRANSACTION
- Use DEFINE ... OVERWRITE for schema evolution
- Apply with `bun migrate` (not manual surreal import)

## Important Notes
- Read schema/surreal-schema.surql to see existing table patterns
- Read an existing migration (e.g. schema/migrations/0029_observer_llm_fields.surql) to match style
- The acceptance tests use createTestLearning() from learning-test-kit.ts which creates records directly via SurrealDB queries - the schema must support those fields
- For this step, focus ONLY on the migration file. Queries and types come in step 01-02.

# TDD_PHASES
Execute in order:
0. PREPARE - Load context, verify prerequisites, read existing migration patterns
1. RED_ACCEPTANCE - The existing acceptance tests in milestone-1 that test schema validation (M1-01 through M1-03, M1-10, M1-11) should be your guide. Since acceptance tests already exist, verify they fail without the migration.
2. RED_UNIT - For a migration-only step, this phase can be skipped (NOT_APPLICABLE: schema migration has no unit-testable code)
3. GREEN - Create the migration file and apply via `bun migrate`. Verify milestone-1 schema tests can create/query records.
4. COMMIT - Stage and commit with conventional message
   Include git trailer: `Step-ID: 01-01`
   Example:
   ```
   feat(learning): add learning table schema migration

   Step-ID: 01-01
   ```

# QUALITY_GATES
- Migration applies cleanly via bun migrate
- No skipped phases without blocked_by reason
- Walking skeleton test infrastructure works (createTestLearning can create records)

# OUTCOME_RECORDING
After ACTUALLY EXECUTING each phase, record via DES CLI:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/agent-learnings \
      --step-id 01-01 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status EXECUTED \
      --data PASS

For SKIPPED phases (genuinely not applicable):

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/agent-learnings \
      --step-id 01-01 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status SKIPPED \
      --data "NOT_APPLICABLE: reason"

# BOUNDARY_RULES
- Only create/modify files listed in implementation scope
- Do not load roadmap.yaml
- Do not modify execution-log.yaml structure (append only via CLI)
- NEVER write execution-log entries for phases you did not execute

# TIMEOUT_INSTRUCTION
Target: 30 turns max. If approaching limit, COMMIT current progress.

TASK BOUNDARY: Implement step 01-01 only. Return control to orchestrator after completion.

**Summary of what was done:**

1. **Created migration** `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/schema/migrations/0030_learning_table.surql` -- SCHEMAFULL `learning` table with all fields, assertions (learning_type, status, source, priority), DEFAULT on priority, HNSW embedding index, workspace+status composite index, plus `learning_evidence` and `supersedes` relation tables.

**Notable finding:** SurrealDB auto-creates `target_agents[*]` when `target_agents` is defined as `array<string>`, so the explicit `DEFINE FIELD target_agents[*]` from the spec caused an "already exists" error. Removed it from both migration and base schema.

## Step 01-01 Complete

**Summary of what was done:**

1. **Created migration** `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/schema/migrations/0030_learning_table.surql` -- SCHEMAFULL `learning` table with all fields, assertions (learning_type, status, source, priority), DEFAULT on priority, HNSW embedding index, workspace+status composite index, plus `learning_evidence` and `supersedes` relation tables.

2. **Updated base schema** `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/schema/surreal-schema.surql` -- added the same learning table definitions so acceptance tests (which apply base schema, not migrations) can run.

3. **Enabled acceptance tests** `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/milestone-1-schema-and-queries.test.ts` -- removed `.skip` from all 11 tests. All 11 pass.

**Notable finding:** SurrealDB auto-creates `target_agents[*]` when `target_agents` is defined as `array<string>`, so the explicit `DEFINE FIELD target_agents[*]` from the spec caused an "already exists" error. Removed it from both migration and base schema.

# DES_METADATA

# SKILL_LOADING
Before starting TDD phases, read your skill files for methodology guidance.

Always load at PREPARE: tdd-methodology.md, quality-framework.md

# TASK_CONTEXT
Name: Learning CRUD queries, types, and shared contracts
Description: Workspace-scoped CRUD operations, status transitions, listing queries, rate-limit counting. Shared contract types in contracts.ts.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. createLearning persists record with evidence edges and returns created learning
2. updateLearningStatus validates workspace scope before transition; supersedeLearning deactivates old and creates supersedes edge
3. listWorkspaceActiveLearnings and listWorkspacePendingLearnings return workspace-filtered results
4. countRecentSuggestionsByAgent returns count of suggestions within last 7 days
5. LearningSummary, LearningType, LearningStatus, LearningSource types exported from shared/contracts.ts; EntityKind union includes learning

Implementation scope (files to create/modify):
- app/src/server/learning/queries.ts (NEW)
- app/src/server/learning/types.ts (NEW)
- app/src/shared/contracts.ts (MODIFY - add learning types)

Test files:
- tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/milestone-1-schema-and-queries.test.ts (existing, tests already enabled from step 01-01)

Dependencies: 01-01 (complete)
Time estimate: 2h

## Architecture Details

Follow the patterns in observation/queries.ts and suggestion/queries.ts for workspace-scoped CRUD.

### queries.ts Functions:
- `createLearning(surreal, workspace, input)` - Create learning + evidence edges via RELATE
- `updateLearningStatus(surreal, workspace, learningId, newStatus, auditFields)` - Validate workspace scope, transition status
- `supersedeLearning(surreal, workspace, oldLearningId, newLearningId, reason)` - Deactivate old + RELATE new->supersedes->old
- `listWorkspaceActiveLearnings(surreal, workspace, agentType?)` - Active learnings filtered by workspace and optional agent type
- `listWorkspacePendingLearnings(surreal, workspace, limit?)` - Pending approval learnings for feed
- `countRecentSuggestionsByAgent(surreal, workspace, agentType)` - Count suggestions in last 7 days for rate limiting

### types.ts - Internal types:
- `CreateLearningInput` - text, learning_type, priority?, target_agents?, source, suggested_by?, pattern_confidence?, created_by?, evidence_ids?
- Re-export shared contract types

### shared/contracts.ts additions:
```typescript
export const LEARNING_TYPES = ["constraint", "instruction", "precedent"] as const;
export type LearningType = (typeof LEARNING_TYPES)[number];

export const LEARNING_STATUSES = ["active", "pending_approval", "dismissed", "superseded", "deactivated"] as const;
export type LearningStatus = (typeof LEARNING_STATUSES)[number];

export const LEARNING_SOURCES = ["human", "agent"] as const;
export type LearningSource = (typeof LEARNING_SOURCES)[number];

export type LearningSummary = {
  id: string;
  text: string;
  learningType: LearningType;
  status: LearningStatus;
  source: LearningSource;
  priority: EntityPriority;
  targetAgents: string[];
  suggestedBy?: string;
  patternConfidence?: number;
  createdAt: string;
  approvedAt?: string;
  dismissedAt?: string;
  dismissedReason?: string;
  deactivatedAt?: string;
};
```

And add `"learning"` to the EntityKind union type.

### Query Patterns (SurrealDB):
- Use RecordId objects, never raw table:id strings
- Two-step KNN for vector queries (SurrealDB HNSW bug)
- No null - use undefined via optional properties
- RELATE for relation edges (not CREATE)
- For supersession: `RELATE new->supersedes->old SET superseded_at = time::now(), reason = $reason`
- Active learnings query filters: workspace + status="active" + (empty target_agents OR agentType IN target_agents)

### Test expectations (from milestone-1 tests):
The tests create records via createTestLearning() helper (direct SurrealDB) and then verify:
- M1-04: listActiveLearnings returns workspace-filtered results
- M1-05: Status transition from active to deactivated records audit trail
- M1-06: Pending approval learning not returned as active
- M1-07: Superseding marks old as superseded and creates edge
- M1-08: Evidence edges link learning to source entities
- M1-09: Workspace isolation
- M1-11: Priority defaults to medium

Note: The milestone-1 tests use the test kit helpers (createTestLearning, listActiveLearnings, etc.) which query SurrealDB directly. The queries.ts functions you create should follow the same query patterns but be called from route handlers (step 02-02). For THIS step, make sure your queries work correctly by verifying the existing acceptance tests still pass.

## Project Conventions
- Always use `--no-verify -s` when committing
- No null in domain data. Use undefined via optional properties.
- Do NOT create wrapper functions for simple operations. Cast directly with `as`.
- Follow functional paradigm: pure functions where possible, effect boundaries at adapter layer.

# TDD_PHASES
Execute in order:
0. PREPARE - Read existing patterns (observation/queries.ts), understand test expectations
1. RED_ACCEPTANCE - Acceptance tests already exist in milestone-1. Verify current state (they should pass for schema-only tests from 01-01). The queries.ts functions will be validated when used by HTTP routes in step 02-02.
2. RED_UNIT - Write unit tests for any pure logic in queries.ts if applicable. If queries are all DB operations, SKIP.
3. GREEN - Create queries.ts, types.ts, modify contracts.ts. Verify milestone-1 tests still pass.
4. COMMIT - Stage and commit with `--no-verify -s`. Include git trailer: `Step-ID: 01-02`

# QUALITY_GATES
- All milestone-1 tests pass
- Types exported correctly from contracts.ts
- No TypeScript errors in new files (run typecheck if available)

# OUTCOME_RECORDING
After ACTUALLY EXECUTING each phase, record via DES CLI:

      --status EXECUTED \
      --data PASS

For SKIPPED phases:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/agent-learnings \
      --step-id 01-02 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status SKIPPED \
      --data "NOT_APPLICABLE: reason"

# RECORDING_INTEGRITY

- Timestamps are generated by the CLI (real UTC), not by you

# BOUNDARY_RULES

- Do not load roadmap.yaml
- Do not modify execution-log.yaml structure (append only via CLI)

## Goal
Implement agent learnings: persistent behavioral rules injected into agent system prompts at runtime (JIT prompting). The system includes schema/CRUD, JIT loading, prompt formatting, prompt injection into 4 agent types, HTTP endpoints with feed integration, pattern detection from conversations/traces/observations, and three-layer collision detection with LLM intent classification.

## Architecture Context (from design artifacts)

The design is fully specified in docs/feature/agent-learnings/design/. Read all files there for full context:
- architecture-design.md (C4 diagrams, component architecture, integration patterns, quality attributes)
- data-models.md (SurrealDB schema, query patterns, types)
- component-boundaries.md (responsibilities, dependencies, testing strategy)
- technology-stack.md (reuse analysis)

Acceptance tests already exist in tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/ — each milestone maps to a step.

### Phase 1: Schema Foundation + Core Domain (3 steps)

**Step one**: Learning table schema and migration
- Migration file: schema/migrations/0030_learning_table.surql
- SCHEMAFULL learning table with all fields from data-models.md
- Relation tables: learning_evidence, supersedes
- Indexes: HNSW on embedding, composite on workspace+status
- Files: schema/migrations/0030_learning_table.surql
- Tests: milestone-1 tests

**Step two**: Learning CRUD queries, types, and shared contract types
- New: app/src/server/learning/queries.ts, app/src/server/learning/types.ts
- Modified: app/src/shared/contracts.ts
- Functions: createLearning, updateLearningStatus, supersedeLearning, listWorkspaceActiveLearnings, listWorkspacePendingLearnings, countRecentSuggestionsByAgent
- Tests: milestone-1 tests

**Step three**: JIT learning loader and prompt formatter
- New: app/src/server/learning/loader.ts, app/src/server/learning/formatter.ts
- Loader with priority sort, token budget (~500), type-based injection rules
- Formatter as pure function, groups by type
- Tests: milestone-2 tests

### Phase 2: Integration + HTTP (2 steps)

**Step four**: Inject learnings into all four prompt builders
- Modified: chat/context.ts, agents/pm/prompt.ts, agents/observer/prompt.ts, mcp/context-builder.ts
- All additive changes
- Tests: milestone-3 tests

**Step five**: Learning HTTP endpoints and feed integration
- New: app/src/server/learning/learning-route.ts
- Modified: start-server.ts, feed/feed-queries.ts, feed/feed-route.ts
- CRUD endpoints + feed cards + audit trail
- Tests: milestone-4 tests

### Phase 3: Agent Self-Improvement + Safety (2 steps)

**Step six**: Pattern detection with rate limiting
- New: app/src/server/learning/detector.ts
- Three detection sources + rate limiting + dismissed prevention
- Tests: milestone-5 tests

**Step seven**: Three-layer collision detection with LLM intent classification
- New: app/src/server/learning/collision.ts
- Three layers with thresholds, LLM classify, severity, fail-open/closed
- Tests: milestone-6 tests

### Implementation Scope
- Source dirs: app/src/server/learning/, app/src/server/chat/, app/src/server/agents/, app/src/server/mcp/, app/src/server/feed/, app/src/shared/, schema/migrations/
- Test dirs: tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/
- Excluded: node_modules/**, dist/**

### Dependencies
- Step one → step two → step three → step four
- Step two → step five
- Step two → step six  
- Step three → step seven
- Step five depends on step three
