1→# Agent Learnings Data Models
     2→
     3→## SurrealDB Schema
     4→
     5→### `learning` Table (SCHEMAFULL)
     6→
     7→```sql
     8→DEFINE TABLE learning SCHEMAFULL;
     9→
    10→-- Core content
    11→DEFINE FIELD text ON learning TYPE string;
    12→DEFINE FIELD learning_type ON learning TYPE string
    13→  ASSERT $value IN ["constraint", "instruction", "precedent"];
    14→DEFINE FIELD status ON learning TYPE string
    15→  ASSERT $value IN ["active", "pending_approval", "dismissed", "superseded", "deactivated"];
    16→DEFINE FIELD source ON learning TYPE string
    17→  ASSERT $value IN ["human", "agent"];
    18→DEFINE FIELD priority ON learning TYPE string DEFAULT "medium"
    19→  ASSERT $value IN ["low", "medium", "high"];
    20→
    21→-- Targeting
    22→DEFINE FIELD target_agents ON learning TYPE array<string>;
    23→DEFINE FIELD target_agents[*] ON learning TYPE string;
    24→DEFINE FIELD workspace ON learning TYPE record<workspace>;
    25→
    26→-- Agent suggestion metadata
    27→DEFINE FIELD suggested_by ON learning TYPE option<string>;
    28→DEFINE FIELD pattern_confidence ON learning TYPE option<float>;
    29→
    30→-- Provenance and audit trail
    31→DEFINE FIELD created_by ON learning TYPE option<record<identity>>;
    32→DEFINE FIELD created_at ON learning TYPE datetime;
    33→DEFINE FIELD updated_at ON learning TYPE option<datetime>;
    34→
    35→-- Approval audit (set when pending_approval -> active)
    36→DEFINE FIELD approved_by ON learning TYPE option<record<identity>>;
    37→DEFINE FIELD approved_at ON learning TYPE option<datetime>;
    38→
    39→-- Activation timestamp (set when status becomes active, regardless of path)
    40→DEFINE FIELD activated_at ON learning TYPE option<datetime>;
    41→
    42→-- Dismissal audit (set when pending_approval -> dismissed)
    43→DEFINE FIELD dismissed_by ON learning TYPE option<record<identity>>;
    44→DEFINE FIELD dismissed_at ON learning TYPE option<datetime>;
    45→DEFINE FIELD dismissed_reason ON learning TYPE option<string>;
    46→
    47→-- Deactivation audit (set when active -> deactivated)
    48→DEFINE FIELD deactivated_by ON learning TYPE option<record<identity>>;
    49→DEFINE FIELD deactivated_at ON learning TYPE option<datetime>;
    50→
    51→-- Vector search
    52→DEFINE FIELD embedding ON learning TYPE option<array<float>>;
    53→
    54→-- Indexes
    55→DEFINE INDEX idx_learning_workspace_status ON learning FIELDS workspace, status;
    56→DEFINE INDEX idx_learning_embedding ON learning FIELDS embedding HNSW DIMENSION 1536 DIST COSINE;
    57→```
    58→
    59→### `learning_evidence` Relation Table
    60→
    61→Links a learning to the evidence that prompted its creation (messages, traces, observations, agent sessions).
    62→
    63→```sql
    64→DEFINE TABLE learning_evidence TYPE RELATION
    65→  IN learning
    66→  OUT message | trace | observation | agent_session
    67→  SCHEMAFULL;
    68→DEFINE FIELD added_at ON learning_evidence TYPE option<datetime>;
    69→```
    70→
    71→### `supersedes` Relation Table
    72→
    73→Links a new learning to the old learning it replaces. Direction: `RELATE new_learning->supersedes->old_learning` (the new learning supersedes the old one; arrow points to what was replaced).
    74→
    75→```sql
    76→DEFINE TABLE supersedes TYPE RELATION
    77→  IN learning
    78→  OUT learning
    79→  SCHEMAFULL;
    80→DEFINE FIELD superseded_at ON supersedes TYPE datetime;
    81→DEFINE FIELD reason ON supersedes TYPE option<string>;
    82→```
    83→
    84→## Status Lifecycle
    85→
    86→```
    87→Human-created:
    88→  -> active (immediate)
    89→  -> active -> superseded (new learning replaces)
    90→  -> active -> deactivated (user disables)
    91→
    92→Agent-suggested:
    93→  -> pending_approval
    94→  -> pending_approval -> active (human approves)
    95→  -> pending_approval -> dismissed (human rejects)
    96→  -> active -> superseded
    97→  -> active -> deactivated
    98→```
    99→
   100→## Learning Types
   101→
   102→| Type | Injection Rule | Example |
   103→|---|---|---|
   104→| `constraint` | ALWAYS injected, never dropped for token budget | "Never use null for domain data values" |
   105→| `instruction` | Injected within token budget, priority-ordered | "Use RecordId objects, never raw table:id strings" |
   106→| `precedent` | Injected only when semantically relevant (similarity > 0.70) | "PostgreSQL chosen over MySQL for JSON support" |
   107→
   108→## Target Agents
   109→
   110→Array of agent type strings. Empty array = all agents.
   111→
   112→Known agent types: `"chat_agent"`, `"pm_agent"`, `"observer_agent"`, `"mcp"`, `"coding_agent"`.
   113→
   114→## Shared Contract Types
   115→
   116→Added to `app/src/shared/contracts.ts`:
   117→
   118→```typescript
   119→export const LEARNING_TYPES = ["constraint", "instruction", "precedent"] as const;
   120→export type LearningType = (typeof LEARNING_TYPES)[number];
   121→
   122→export const LEARNING_STATUSES = ["active", "pending_approval", "dismissed", "superseded", "deactivated"] as const;
   123→export type LearningStatus = (typeof LEARNING_STATUSES)[number];
   124→
   125→export const LEARNING_SOURCES = ["human", "agent"] as const;
   126→export type LearningSource = (typeof LEARNING_SOURCES)[number];
   127→
   128→export type LearningSummary = {
   129→  id: string;
   130→  text: string;
   131→  learningType: LearningType;
   132→  status: LearningStatus;
   133→  source: LearningSource;
   134→  priority: EntityPriority;
   135→  targetAgents: string[];
   136→  suggestedBy?: string;
   137→  patternConfidence?: number;
   138→  createdAt: string;
   139→  approvedAt?: string;
   140→  dismissedAt?: string;
   141→  dismissedReason?: string;
   142→  deactivatedAt?: string;
   143→};
   144→```
   145→
   146→## Collision Detection Types
   147→
   148→```typescript
   149→export type CollisionType = "contradicts" | "duplicates" | "overlaps" | "reinforces";
   150→
   151→export type CollisionResult = {
   152→  collisionType: CollisionType;
   153→  targetKind: "learning" | "policy" | "decision";
   154→  targetId: string;
   155→  targetText: string;
   156→  similarity: number;
   157→  blocking: boolean; // true for policy collisions
   158→};
   159→```
   160→
   161→## Query Patterns
   162→
   163→### Load Active Learnings (JIT - follows two-step KNN pattern)
   164→
   165→For `constraint` and `instruction` types (non-semantic):
   166→```sql
   167→SELECT id, text, learning_type, source, priority, target_agents, created_at
   168→FROM learning
   169→WHERE workspace = $workspace
   170→  AND status = "active"
   171→  AND learning_type IN ["constraint", "instruction"]
   172→  AND (array::len(target_agents) = 0 OR $agentType IN target_agents)
   173→ORDER BY
   174→  source = "human" DESC,
   175→  priority = "high" DESC,
   176→  priority = "medium" DESC,
   177→  created_at DESC
   178→LIMIT 50;
   179→```
   180→
   181→For `precedent` type (semantic - two-step KNN):
   182→```sql
   183→-- Step 1: KNN candidates
   184→LET $candidates = SELECT id, text, learning_type, source, priority, target_agents, created_at,
   185→    vector::similarity::cosine(embedding, $contextEmbedding) AS similarity
   186→  FROM learning
   187→  WHERE embedding <|20, COSINE|> $contextEmbedding;
   188→
   189→-- Step 2: Filter by workspace + status + relevance
   190→SELECT * FROM $candidates
   191→  WHERE workspace = $workspace
   192→  AND status = "active"
   193→  AND learning_type = "precedent"
   194→  AND (array::len(target_agents) = 0 OR $agentType IN target_agents)
   195→  AND similarity > 0.70
   196→  ORDER BY similarity DESC
   197→  LIMIT 10;
   198→```
   199→
   200→### Collision Check (similarity search)
   201→
   202→```sql
   203→-- Step 1: KNN candidates from active learnings
   204→LET $candidates = SELECT id, text, learning_type,
   205→    vector::similarity::cosine(embedding, $newEmbedding) AS similarity
   206→  FROM learning
   207→  WHERE embedding <|20, COSINE|> $newEmbedding;
   208→
   209→-- Step 2: Filter
   210→SELECT * FROM $candidates
   211→  WHERE workspace = $workspace
   212→  AND status = "active"
   213→  AND similarity > 0.75
   214→  ORDER BY similarity DESC
   215→  LIMIT 10;
   216→```
   217→
   218→### Pending Learnings for Feed
   219→
   220→```sql
   221→SELECT id, text, learning_type, source, status, suggested_by,
   222→       pattern_confidence, target_agents, created_at
   223→FROM learning
   224→WHERE workspace = $workspace
   225→  AND status = "pending_approval"
   226→ORDER BY created_at DESC
   227→LIMIT $limit;
   228→```
   229→
   230→### Rate Limiting Check
   231→
   232→```sql
   233→SELECT count() AS count
   234→FROM learning
   235→WHERE workspace = $workspace
   236→  AND suggested_by = $agentType
   237→  AND created_at > time::now() - 7d
   238→GROUP ALL;
   239→```
   240→
   241→Detector logic: if count >= 5, skip suggestion creation and log observation "Learning suggestion rate limit reached for {agentType} ({count}/5 this week)".
   242→
   243→### Dismissed Re-Suggestion Prevention (two-step KNN)
   244→
   245→Before creating an agent-suggested learning, check if a similar learning was previously dismissed:
   246→
   247→```sql
   248→-- Step 1: KNN candidates from dismissed learnings
   249→LET $dismissed_candidates = SELECT id, text,
   250→    vector::similarity::cosine(embedding, $proposedEmbedding) AS similarity
   251→  FROM learning
   252→  WHERE embedding <|10, COSINE|> $proposedEmbedding;
   253→
   254→-- Step 2: Filter by workspace + dismissed status + high similarity
   255→SELECT * FROM $dismissed_candidates
   256→  WHERE workspace = $workspace
   257→  AND status = "dismissed"
   258→  AND similarity > 0.85
   259→  LIMIT 1;
   260→```
   261→
   262→If any row returned: skip suggestion creation. The human already rejected a substantially similar learning.
   263→
   264→## Graph Relationships
   265→
   266→```
   267→learning --[learning_evidence]--> message | trace | observation | agent_session
   268→learning --[supersedes]--> learning (the superseded one)
   269→```
   270→
   271→No `belongs_to` edge to project/feature -- learnings are workspace-scoped, not project-scoped. This is intentional: a learning like "never use null" applies across all projects.
   272→

1→# Agent Learnings Component Boundaries
     2→
     3→## Domain Boundary
     4→
     5→The learning domain lives under `app/src/server/learning/` as a self-contained module, following the same pattern as `observation/`, `suggestion/`, and `intent/`.
     6→
     7→## Component Dependency Map
     8→
     9→```
    10→                    +-----------------+
    11→                    |  HTTP Adapter   |
    12→                    | learning-route  |
    13→                    +--------+--------+
    14→                             |
    15→              +--------------+--------------+
    16→              |              |              |
    17→    +---------v--+    +------v------+  +----v-------+
    18→    |  queries   |    | collision   |  | detector   |
    19→    | (CRUD/DB)  |    | (safety)   |  | (patterns) |
    20→    +-----+------+    +------+------+  +-----+------+
    21→          |                  |               |
    22→          |           +------v------+        |
    23→          |           |   loader    |        |
    24→          |           | (JIT read)  |        |
    25→          |           +------+------+        |
    26→          |                  |               |
    27→          |           +------v------+        |
    28→          |           | formatter   |        |
    29→          |           | (pure text) |        |
    30→          |           +-------------+        |
    31→          |                                  |
    32→    +-----v----------------------------------v-----+
    33→    |              SurrealDB + LLM Provider        |
    34→    +----------------------------------------------+
    35→```
    36→
    37→## Component Responsibilities
    38→
    39→### `queries.ts` - Learning Persistence
    40→
    41→**Owns**: All SurrealDB read/write operations for the `learning` table and its relation edges.
    42→
    43→**Functions**:
    44→- `createLearning` - Create learning record + evidence edges
    45→- `updateLearningStatus` - Transition status (with workspace scope validation)
    46→- `supersedeLearning` - Deactivate old + create supersedes edge
    47→- `listWorkspaceActiveLearnings` - Active learnings by workspace, optional agent type filter
    48→- `listWorkspacePendingLearnings` - Pending approval learnings for feed
    49→- `countRecentSuggestionsByAgent` - Rate limiting check
    50→
    51→**Dependencies**: SurrealDB (driven port)
    52→
    53→**Follows pattern of**: `observation/queries.ts`, `suggestion/queries.ts`
    54→
    55→### `loader.ts` - JIT Learning Loading
    56→
    57→**Owns**: Priority sorting, type-based filtering, token budgeting for prompt injection.
    58→
    59→**Functions**:
    60→- `loadActiveLearnings` - Main entry point. Takes `(surreal, workspaceRecord, agentType, contextEmbedding?)`. Returns sorted, budget-trimmed learning list.
    61→- `estimateTokens` - Token estimation. Takes text string, returns estimated token count via `Math.ceil(text.trim().split(/\s+/).length / 0.75)`.
    62→
    63→**Dependencies**: `queries.ts` (for DB access)
    64→
    65→**Key behaviors**:
    66→- Constraints: always included, never trimmed. If constraints alone exceed budget, log observation but include all.
    67→- Instructions: included in priority order within remaining budget (skip oversized, try next)
    68→- Precedents: only included when `contextEmbedding` provided and similarity > 0.70. If `contextEmbedding` is undefined, precedents are excluded entirely (not an error).
    69→- Token budget: ~500 tokens (~375 words) via word-count heuristic (ADR-026)
    70→
    71→**Context embedding source per prompt builder**:
    72→
    73→| Prompt Builder | `contextEmbedding` Source | Precedent Behavior |
    74→|---|---|---|
    75→| Chat agent (`chat/context.ts`) | Embedding of conversation's project description (if project linked) | Excluded if no project linked |
    76→| PM agent (`agents/pm/prompt.ts`) | Embedding of project description | Excluded if project has no description |
    77→| MCP context (`mcp/context-builder.ts`) | Embedding of task description from context packet request | Excluded if no task scope |
    78→| Observer (`agents/observer/prompt.ts`) | Not provided | Precedents always excluded -- observer gets constraints + instructions only |
    79→
    80→### `formatter.ts` - Prompt Section Rendering (Pure)
    81→
    82→**Owns**: Converting learning records into prompt text sections.
    83→
    84→**Functions**:
    85→- `formatLearningsSection` - Takes learning array, returns formatted string with type grouping
    86→
    87→**Dependencies**: None (pure function, no IO)
    88→
    89→**Output format**:
    90→```
    91→## Workspace Learnings
    92→### Constraints (always apply)
    93→- [text]
    94→### Instructions
    95→- [text]
    96→### Precedents (contextually relevant)
    97→- [text]
    98→```
    99→
   100→### `collision.ts` - Collision Detection
   101→
   102→**Owns**: Three-layer collision check before learning activation.
   103→
   104→**Functions**:
   105→- `checkCollisions` - Takes new learning text, embedding (or undefined), workspace, and LLM model. Returns collision results array.
   106→- `classifyIntent` - LLM structured output call. Takes two texts and target kind. Returns `{ classification, reasoning }`.
   107→
   108→**Dependencies**: SurrealDB (driven port), LLM (driven port for intent classification)
   109→
   110→**Layers** (see ADR-028 for unified classification algorithm):
   111→1. Learning-vs-learning: similarity > 0.90 = duplicate; 0.75-0.90 = LLM classify; < 0.75 = no collision
   112→2. Learning-vs-policy: similarity > 0.80 = LLM classify (contradiction = hard block); < 0.80 = no collision
   113→3. Learning-vs-decision: similarity > 0.80 = LLM classify (contradiction = informational); < 0.80 = no collision
   114→
   115→**LLM fallback**: If LLM unavailable, default to `"contradicts"` (safer -- surfaces warning rather than missing conflict).
   116→
   117→**Embedding unavailable behavior**:
   118→- Human-created learning: skip collision check entirely, activate learning, log observation "Learning activated without collision check -- embedding service unavailable"
   119→- Agent-suggested learning: skip collision check, status stays `pending_approval` anyway (human will review)
   120→- Deferred check: Observer picks up learnings with missing embeddings on next scan, computes embedding, runs retroactive collision check
   121→
   122→### `detector.ts` - Pattern Detection
   123→
   124→**Owns**: Identifying correction patterns across three data sources.
   125→
   126→**Functions**:
   127→- `detectConversationCorrections` - Scans recent messages for repeated corrections on same topic
   128→- `detectTraceFailures` - Scans trace table for repeated tool failures
   129→- `detectObservationClusters` - Clusters observations by embedding similarity
   130→
   131→**Dependencies**: SurrealDB (driven port), LLM (driven port for correction extraction)
   132→
   133→**Used by**:
   134→- Chat agent: calls `detectConversationCorrections()` synchronously during message processing
   135→- Observer agent: calls `detectTraceFailures()` + `detectObservationClusters()` during scheduled graph scan (configurable frequency, default every 6 hours)
   136→
   137→**Rate limiting gate** (executed before any suggestion creation):
   138→1. Call `countRecentSuggestionsByAgent(surreal, workspace, agentType)` -- if count >= 5, log observation and skip
   139→2. Call dismissed re-suggestion check (KNN on dismissed learnings, similarity > 0.85) -- if match found, skip
   140→
   141→**Embedding error handling**: If embedding generation fails for a proposed learning text, create the learning record with `embedding: undefined`. The Observer's deferred scan will compute the embedding later.
   142→
   143→### `learning-route.ts` - HTTP Adapter
   144→
   145→**Owns**: HTTP endpoint registration for learning CRUD.
   146→
   147→**Endpoints**:
   148→- `POST /api/workspaces/:workspaceId/learnings` - Create learning
   149→- `GET /api/workspaces/:workspaceId/learnings` - List learnings (filterable by status, type, agent)
   150→- `POST /api/workspaces/:workspaceId/learnings/:learningId/actions` - Status transitions (approve, dismiss, deactivate, supersede)
   151→
   152→**Dependencies**: `queries.ts`, `collision.ts`, embedding model, `deps.inflight`
   153→
   154→**Follows pattern of**: `entities/entity-actions-route.ts`, `feed/feed-route.ts`
   155→
   156→**Inflight tracking** (per AGENTS.md): When the route handler fires background work (embedding generation, deferred collision check after embedding retry), wrap with `deps.inflight.track(promise)`. This ensures smoke tests can `drain()` pending work before closing connections.
   157→
   158→**Embedding error handling in create flow**:
   159→1. Attempt embedding generation for the learning text
   160→2. If embedding succeeds: persist learning with embedding, run collision check synchronously, return result with collisions
   161→3. If embedding fails: persist learning with `embedding: undefined`, skip collision check, log observation "Learning created without embedding -- collision check deferred", return result with `collisionCheckDeferred: true`
   162→4. The actions endpoint (approve) also attempts collision check before activation. If embedding is still missing at approve time, compute it then. If that also fails, human-created learnings activate anyway (fail-open), agent-suggested learnings are blocked with error.
   163→
   164→### `types.ts` - Internal Types
   165→
   166→**Owns**: Internal type definitions not shared with client.
   167→
   168→**Contains**: DB row types, collision internal types, detector config types.
   169→
   170→## Dependency Inversion Compliance
   171→
   172→All components depend inward (toward domain logic):
   173→
   174→- **HTTP adapter** (`learning-route.ts`) depends on domain (`queries.ts`, `collision.ts`)
   175→- **Domain** depends on driven ports (SurrealDB, LLM) via function parameters, not module-level imports
   176→- **Pure functions** (`formatter.ts`) have zero dependencies
   177→- **Integration points** (chat context, PM prompt, etc.) call `loadActiveLearnings()` and `formatLearningsSection()` -- one-way dependency into learning domain
   178→
   179→No circular dependencies. No learning domain code depends on chat, PM, observer, or MCP modules.
   180→
   181→## Testing Strategy
   182→
   183→| Component | Test Type | Strategy |
   184→|---|---|---|
   185→| `formatter.ts` | Unit | Pure function. Input: learning array. Assert: grouped output text, type ordering, empty handling. |
   186→| `loader.ts` (sort + budget) | Unit | Pure sort/budget logic. Input: unsorted learnings + budget. Assert: constraint-first ordering, budget trimming, overflow observation. |
   187→| `loader.ts` (DB integration) | Acceptance | Seed learnings in test DB. Assert: workspace filtering, agent type filtering, precedent similarity threshold. |
   188→| `collision.ts` | Acceptance | Seed active learnings + policies + decisions with embeddings. Assert: duplicate detection, LLM classification dispatch, policy hard block, fail-open behavior. Mock LLM for deterministic classification. |
   189→| `detector.ts` | Acceptance | Seed conversation history / traces / observations. Assert: correction pattern detection, rate limiting, dismissed re-suggestion prevention. Mock LLM for correction extraction. |
   190→| `learning-route.ts` | Acceptance | Full HTTP flow via `acceptance-test-kit.ts`. Assert: create with embedding, list with filters, approve/dismiss/deactivate actions, feed card appearance. |
   191→| Prompt injection | Acceptance | Create active learning, build system prompt, assert "Workspace Learnings" section present. Per prompt builder: chat, PM, observer, MCP. |
   192→

   237→### Feed Integration
   238→```
   239→feed/feed-queries.ts gains:
   240→  - listPendingLearnings() query function
   241→  - mapPendingLearningToFeedItem() mapper
   242→
   243→feed/feed-route.ts gains:
   244→  - Pending learnings added to review tier
   245→  - Learning actions: approve, dismiss, discuss
   246→```
   247→

1→# Agent Learnings Technology Stack
     2→
     3→## No New Technologies Required
     4→
     5→Agent Learnings is implemented entirely within Brain's existing technology stack. No new dependencies, services, or infrastructure components are introduced.
     6→
     7→| Concern | Technology | License | Rationale |
     8→|---|---|---|---|
     9→| Persistence | SurrealDB (existing) | BSL 1.1 | Already in use. SCHEMAFULL table, HNSW index for embeddings, TYPE RELATION for evidence edges. |
    10→| Vector similarity | SurrealDB HNSW + cosine (existing) | BSL 1.1 | Collision detection and precedent relevance use existing vector infrastructure. Two-step KNN pattern per codebase constraint. |
    11→| Embedding generation | Existing embedding model via AI SDK (existing) | N/A | Same `createEmbeddingVector()` used by observation, suggestion, entity embeddings. |
    12→| LLM reasoning | Existing extraction/observer model via AI SDK (existing) | N/A | Pattern detection reuses existing model wiring. No new model dependency. |
    13→| HTTP layer | Bun.serve (existing) | MIT | New route handlers registered in `start-server.ts`, same pattern as feed, entity, observer routes. |
    14→| Token estimation | Word-count heuristic (new, zero-dependency) | N/A | ~500 token budget estimated as ~375 words. No tokenizer library needed -- precision not critical for prompt budget. |
    15→| Type contracts | TypeScript (existing) | Apache-2.0 | Shared types in `contracts.ts`, internal types in `learning/types.ts`. |
    16→
    17→## Decision: Word-Count Heuristic over Tokenizer Library
    18→
    19→See ADR-026 for full rationale. Summary: exact tokenization adds a dependency (tiktoken, ~2MB WASM) for marginal precision improvement on a soft budget. Word-count heuristic (1 word ~= 1.33 tokens) is sufficient for the ~500 token learning injection budget.
    20→
    21→## Decision: New Table over Extending Suggestion Table
    22→
    23→See ADR-027 for full rationale. Summary: learnings have a fundamentally different lifecycle (active/superseded/deactivated vs pending/accepted/dismissed/converted), different schema (target_agents, learning_type, priority), and different consumption pattern (JIT prompt injection vs feed card governance). Extending suggestion would require discriminated union fields and complex status machinery.
    24→
    25→## Reuse Analysis
    26→
    27→| Existing Component | Reuse | Justification |
    28→|---|---|---|
    29→| `observation/queries.ts` pattern | Pattern reuse | Learning queries follow same workspace-scoped CRUD pattern |
    30→| `suggestion/queries.ts` pattern | Pattern reuse | Status transition pattern (validate workspace scope, update status, set timestamp) |
    31→| `graph/embeddings.ts` | Direct reuse | `createEmbeddingVector()` for learning embeddings |
    32→| `feed/feed-queries.ts` pattern | Pattern reuse | Pending learning query + feed item mapper |
    33→| `feed/feed-route.ts` pattern | Extension | Add pending learnings to existing review tier |
    34→| `entities/entity-actions-route.ts` pattern | Pattern reuse | Learning action handler follows same action dispatch pattern |
    35→| `shared/contracts.ts` | Extension | Add learning types to existing contracts |
    36→| `chat/context.ts` | Extension | Add learnings to ChatContext and buildSystemPrompt |
    37→| `agents/pm/prompt.ts` | Extension | Add learnings section |
    38→| `agents/observer/prompt.ts` | Extension | Add learnings section |
    39→| `mcp/context-builder.ts` | Extension | Add learnings to context packets |
    40→

<!-- DES-VALIDATION : required -->
<!-- DES-PROJECT-ID : agent-learnings -->
<!-- DES-STEP-ID : 01-01 -->

# DES_METADATA
Step: 01-01
Project: agent-learnings
Command: /nw:execute

# AGENT_IDENTITY
Agent: nw-functional-software-crafter

# SKILL_LOADING
Before starting TDD phases, read your skill files for methodology guidance.
Skills path: ~/.claude/skills/nw/nw-functional-software-crafter/
Always load at PREPARE: tdd-methodology.md, quality-framework.md
Load on-demand per phase as specified in your Skill Loading Strategy table.

# TASK_CONTEXT
Name: Learning table schema and migration
Description: SCHEMAFULL learning table with full lifecycle fields, audit trail, relation tables (learning_evidence, supersedes), and indexes.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. Learning table defines all fields: text, learning_type, status, source, priority, target_agents, workspace, embedding, and full audit trail (approved_by/at, dismissed_by/at/reason, deactivated_by/at, activated_at)
2. learning_evidence TYPE RELATION IN learning OUT message|trace|observation|agent_session with added_at field
3. supersedes TYPE RELATION IN learning OUT learning with superseded_at and reason fields
4. HNSW index on embedding (DIMENSION 1536 DIST COSINE), composite index on workspace+status
5. Migration applies cleanly via bun migrate with no errors

Implementation scope (files to create/modify):
- schema/migrations/0030_learning_table.surql (NEW)

Test files:
- tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/milestone-1-schema-and-queries.test.ts (existing, has .skip tests)

Dependencies: None (first step)
Time estimate: 1h

## Key Schema Details (from data-models.md)

```sql
DEFINE TABLE learning SCHEMAFULL;
DEFINE FIELD text ON learning TYPE string;
DEFINE FIELD learning_type ON learning TYPE string ASSERT $value IN ["constraint", "instruction", "precedent"];
DEFINE FIELD status ON learning TYPE string ASSERT $value IN ["active", "pending_approval", "dismissed", "superseded", "deactivated"];
DEFINE FIELD source ON learning TYPE string ASSERT $value IN ["human", "agent"];
DEFINE FIELD priority ON learning TYPE string DEFAULT "medium" ASSERT $value IN ["low", "medium", "high"];
DEFINE FIELD target_agents ON learning TYPE array<string>;
DEFINE FIELD target_agents[*] ON learning TYPE string;
DEFINE FIELD workspace ON learning TYPE record<workspace>;
DEFINE FIELD suggested_by ON learning TYPE option<string>;
DEFINE FIELD pattern_confidence ON learning TYPE option<float>;
DEFINE FIELD created_by ON learning TYPE option<record<identity>>;
DEFINE FIELD created_at ON learning TYPE datetime;
DEFINE FIELD updated_at ON learning TYPE option<datetime>;
DEFINE FIELD approved_by ON learning TYPE option<record<identity>>;
DEFINE FIELD approved_at ON learning TYPE option<datetime>;
DEFINE FIELD activated_at ON learning TYPE option<datetime>;
DEFINE FIELD dismissed_by ON learning TYPE option<record<identity>>;
DEFINE FIELD dismissed_at ON learning TYPE option<datetime>;
DEFINE FIELD dismissed_reason ON learning TYPE option<string>;
DEFINE FIELD deactivated_by ON learning TYPE option<record<identity>>;
DEFINE FIELD deactivated_at ON learning TYPE option<datetime>;
DEFINE FIELD embedding ON learning TYPE option<array<float>>;
DEFINE INDEX idx_learning_workspace_status ON learning FIELDS workspace, status;
DEFINE INDEX idx_learning_embedding ON learning FIELDS embedding HNSW DIMENSION 1536 DIST COSINE;

DEFINE TABLE learning_evidence TYPE RELATION IN learning OUT message | trace | observation | agent_session SCHEMAFULL;
DEFINE FIELD added_at ON learning_evidence TYPE option<datetime>;

DEFINE TABLE supersedes TYPE RELATION IN learning OUT learning SCHEMAFULL;
DEFINE FIELD superseded_at ON supersedes TYPE datetime;
DEFINE FIELD reason ON supersedes TYPE option<string>;
```

## Project Conventions (from AGENTS.md)
- Always use `--no-verify` when committing. Always use `-s` (GPG sign) when committing.
- Migration filenames: zero-padded autoincrement prefix + underscore + slug
- Wrap migrations in BEGIN TRANSACTION; ... COMMIT TRANSACTION;
- DEFINE ANALYZER cannot run inside transactions - place before BEGIN TRANSACTION
- Apply with `bun migrate`

## Important Notes
- Read schema/surreal-schema.surql to see existing table patterns
- Read an existing migration (e.g. schema/migrations/0029_observer_llm_fields.surql) to match style
- The acceptance tests use createTestLearning() from learning-test-kit.ts which creates records directly via SurrealDB queries
- For this step, focus ONLY on the migration file

<!-- DES-VALIDATION : required -->

Step: 01-01

Load on-demand per phase as specified in your Skill Loading Strategy table.

# TASK_CONTEXT
Name: Learning table schema and migration
Description: SCHEMAFULL learning table with full lifecycle fields, audit trail, relation tables (learning_evidence, supersedes), and indexes.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. Learning table defines all fields: text, learning_type, status, source, priority, target_agents, workspace, embedding, and full audit trail (approved_by/at, dismissed_by/at/reason, deactivated_by/at, activated_at)
2. learning_evidence TYPE RELATION IN learning OUT message|trace|observation|agent_session with added_at field
3. supersedes TYPE RELATION IN learning OUT learning with superseded_at and reason fields
4. HNSW index on embedding (DIMENSION 1536 DIST COSINE), composite index on workspace+status
5. Migration applies cleanly via bun migrate with no errors

Implementation scope (files to create/modify):
- schema/migrations/0030_learning_table.surql (NEW)

Test files:
- tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/milestone-1-schema-and-queries.test.ts (existing, has .skip tests)

Dependencies: None (first step)
Time estimate: 1h

## Key Schema Details (from data-models.md)

```sql
DEFINE TABLE learning SCHEMAFULL;
DEFINE FIELD text ON learning TYPE string;
DEFINE FIELD learning_type ON learning TYPE string ASSERT $value IN ["constraint", "instruction", "precedent"];
DEFINE FIELD status ON learning TYPE string ASSERT $value IN ["active", "pending_approval", "dismissed", "superseded", "deactivated"];
DEFINE FIELD source ON learning TYPE string ASSERT $value IN ["human", "agent"];
DEFINE FIELD priority ON learning TYPE string DEFAULT "medium" ASSERT $value IN ["low", "medium", "high"];
DEFINE FIELD target_agents ON learning TYPE array<string>;
DEFINE FIELD target_agents[*] ON learning TYPE string;
DEFINE FIELD workspace ON learning TYPE record<workspace>;
DEFINE FIELD suggested_by ON learning TYPE option<string>;
DEFINE FIELD pattern_confidence ON learning TYPE option<float>;
DEFINE FIELD created_by ON learning TYPE option<record<identity>>;
DEFINE FIELD created_at ON learning TYPE datetime;
DEFINE FIELD updated_at ON learning TYPE option<datetime>;
DEFINE FIELD approved_by ON learning TYPE option<record<identity>>;
DEFINE FIELD approved_at ON learning TYPE option<datetime>;
DEFINE FIELD activated_at ON learning TYPE option<datetime>;
DEFINE FIELD dismissed_by ON learning TYPE option<record<identity>>;
DEFINE FIELD dismissed_at ON learning TYPE option<datetime>;
DEFINE FIELD dismissed_reason ON learning TYPE option<string>;
DEFINE FIELD deactivated_by ON learning TYPE option<record<identity>>;
DEFINE FIELD deactivated_at ON learning TYPE option<datetime>;
DEFINE FIELD embedding ON learning TYPE option<array<float>>;
DEFINE INDEX idx_learning_workspace_status ON learning FIELDS workspace, status;
DEFINE INDEX idx_learning_embedding ON learning FIELDS embedding HNSW DIMENSION 1536 DIST COSINE;

DEFINE TABLE learning_evidence TYPE RELATION IN learning OUT message | trace | observation | agent_session SCHEMAFULL;
DEFINE FIELD added_at ON learning_evidence TYPE option<datetime>;

DEFINE TABLE supersedes TYPE RELATION IN learning OUT learning SCHEMAFULL;
DEFINE FIELD superseded_at ON supersedes TYPE datetime;
DEFINE FIELD reason ON supersedes TYPE option<string>;
```

tlaplus-verification.md

1→---
     2→name: tdd-methodology
     3→description: Deep knowledge for Outside-In TDD - double-loop architecture, ATDD integration, port-to-port testing, walking skeletons, and test doubles policy
     4→---
     5→
     6→# Outside-In TDD Methodology
     7→
     8→## Double-Loop TDD Architecture
     9→
    10→Outer loop: ATDD/E2E Tests (customer view) - business requirements, hours-days to green.
    11→Inner loop: Unit Tests (developer view) - technical implementation, minutes to green, RED->GREEN->REFACTOR.
    12→
    13→Outer stays red while inner cycles. Outer drives WHAT to build, inner drives HOW.
    14→Never build components not needed by actual user scenarios.
    15→
    16→## Outside-In vs Inside-Out
    17→
    18→Inside-Out (Classic/bottom-up): discovers collaborators through refactoring. TDD guides design completely.
    19→Outside-In (London/top-down/mockist): knows collaborators upfront, mocks them, implements each moving inward.
    20→
    21→Use Outside-In when: architectural boundaries known (hexagonal), program to interface not implementation.
    22→
    23→## ATDD Integration (Lightweight)
    24→
    25→Original 2008 heavyweight ATDD was "too heavyweight for most real teams." Updated approach (Hendrickson 2024):
    26→- Few Given/When/Then examples, not many | Separate requirements from tests
    27→- Smallest subset of team with relevant skills | Value = shared understanding, not executable specs
    28→- Automate only where high-value
    29→
    30→## BDD Integration
    31→
    32→BDD emerged from Outside-In TDD. Given(context)->When(action)->Then(outcome) maps to outside-in mindset.
    33→BDD reframes TDD as design/specification technique, not just testing. More accessible to stakeholders.
    34→Gherkin: structured format bridging technical/non-technical. Use pragmatically - automate only where high value.
    35→
    36→## Outside-In Development Workflow (Bache)
    37→1. Write Guiding Test (acceptance) from user perspective - thick slice of functionality
    38→2. Start at top-level entry point, design collaborating classes incrementally
    39→3. Use mocks to experiment with interfaces/protocols
    40→4. As each layer implemented, move to previously mocked collaborators, TDD again
    41→5. Never build what isn't needed for actual user scenarios
    42→
    43→## Port-to-Port Testing
    44→
    45→Tests enter through driving port (application service / public API) and assert outcomes at driven port boundaries.
    46→Internal classes (entities, value objects, domain services) exercised indirectly - never instantiated directly in test code.
    47→
    48→Flow: Driving Port -> Application -> Domain -> Driven Port (mocked)
    49→
    50→```python
    51→def test_order_service_processes_payment():
    52→    # Setup - mock driven port (external dependency)
    53→    payment_gateway = MockPaymentGateway()
    54→    order_repo = InMemoryOrderRepository()
    55→
    56→    # Test through driving port (application service)
    57→    order_service = OrderService(payment_gateway, order_repo)
    58→    result = order_service.place_order(customer_id, items)
    59→
    60→    # Assert observable outcomes
    61→    assert result.is_confirmed()
    62→    payment_gateway.verify_charge_called(amount=100.00)
    63→```
    64→
    65→## Unit of Behavior (not Unit of Code)
    66→
    67→Test = story about the problem your code solves. Granularity related to stakeholder needs.
    68→A unit of behavior may span multiple classes. Test from driving port to driven port boundary.
    69→Key question: "Can you explain this test to a stakeholder?" If not, you're testing implementation details.
    70→
    71→## Classical vs Mockist Verification
    72→
    73→Classical TDD: real objects | state verification | less coupled to implementation | survives refactoring better.
    74→Mockist TDD: mocks for objects with behavior | behavior verification | lighter setup | more coupled to impl.
    75→Best practice: combine strategically. Behavior verification at layer boundaries, state verification within layers.
    76→
    77→## Test Doubles Taxonomy (Meszaros)
    78→- Dummy: passed but never used
    79→- Fake: working impl with shortcuts (in-memory DB)
    80→- Stub: predefined answers
    81→- Spy: stub that records interactions
    82→- Mock: pre-programmed with expectations for behavior verification
    83→
    84→Choose type by need: mock for interaction design | stub when don't care about interaction | fake for integration bridge.
    85→
    86→## Hexagonal Architecture Testing Strategy
    87→
    88→### Domain Layer
    89→Tested indirectly through driving port (application service) unit tests with real domain objects.
    90→Domain entities, value objects, domain services are implementation details. Testing them directly couples tests to internal structure.
    91→
    92→Exception: Standalone domain logic with complex algorithms (e.g., pricing engine) MAY be tested directly when complexity warrants it and the class has a stable public interface. This is the EXCEPTION, not the rule.
    93→
    94→### Application Layer
    95→Classical TDD within layer, Mockist TDD at port boundaries.
    96→Use real Order, Money, Customer objects in application service tests.
    97→Mock IPaymentGateway, IEmailService ports when testing orchestration.
    98→
    99→### Infrastructure Layer
   100→Integration tests ONLY - no unit tests for adapters.
   101→Mocking infrastructure inside an adapter test is testing the mock, not the adapter.
   102→Use real infrastructure (testcontainers, in-memory databases) to verify actual behavior.
   103→
   104→### E2E Tests
   105→Minimal mocking - only truly external systems (3rd party APIs beyond your control).
   106→Use real domain services, application services, repositories.
   107→
   108→## Test Doubles Policy
   109→
   110→Acceptable (port boundaries only):
   111→- `Mock<IPaymentGateway>` - external payment service port
   112→- `Mock<IEmailService>` - external email provider port
   113→- `InMemoryUserRepository` - fake for fast tests (implements IUserRepository port)
   114→
   115→Do not mock inside the hexagon:
   116→- Domain entities (Order, Customer) - use real objects
   117→- Value objects (Money, Email) - cheap to create, deterministic
   118→- Application services (OrderProcessor) - use real with mocked ports
   119→- Domain services (PricingService) - use real objects
   120→
   121→## Walking Skeleton Protocol
   122→
   123→At most one walking skeleton per new feature. When `is_walking_skeleton: true` in roadmap:
   124→- Write exactly ONE E2E/acceptance test proving end-to-end wiring
   125→- Implement thinnest possible slice - hardcoded values, minimal branching
   126→- Do NOT write unit tests - the E2E test IS the deliverable
   127→- Do NOT add error handling, edge cases, or validation
   128→- Skip inner TDD loop, go directly from RED_ACCEPTANCE to GREEN
   129→
   130→E2E tests are inherently slow and flaky. The walking skeleton is the ONE justified E2E test per feature.
   131→
   132→## E2E Test Management
   133→
   134→Enable ONE E2E test at a time to prevent commit blocks:
   135→1. All E2E tests except first one marked with skip/ignore
   136→2. Complete first scenario through Outside-In TDD
   137→3. Commit working implementation
   138→4. Enable next E2E test
   139→5. Repeat until all scenarios implemented
   140→
   141→## Step Method Pattern
   142→
   143→Step methods call production services, not test infrastructure:
   144→
   145→```csharp
   146→[When("business action occurs")]
   147→public async Task WhenBusinessActionOccurs()
   148→{
   149→    var service = _serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<IBusinessService>();
   150→    _result = await service.PerformBusinessActionAsync(_testData);
   151→}
   152→```
   153→
   154→Scaffold unimplemented collaborators with `NotImplementedException`:
   155→```csharp
   156→throw new NotImplementedException(
   157→    "Business capability not yet implemented - driven by outside-in TDD"
   158→);
   159→```
   160→
   161→## Business-Focused Testing
   162→
   163→### Unit Test Naming
   164→- Class pattern: `<DrivingPort>Should`
   165→- Method pattern: `<ExpectedOutcome>_When<SpecificBehavior>[_Given<Preconditions>]`
   166→- Example: `AccountServiceShould.IncreaseBalance_WhenDepositMade_GivenSufficientFunds`
   167→
   168→### Behavior Types
   169→- Command behavior: changes system state (Given-When-Then)
   170→- Query behavior: returns state projection (Given-Then)
   171→- Process behavior: orchestrates multiple commands/queries
   172→
   173→### Test Structure
   174→- Arrange: set up business context and test data
   175→- Act: perform business action
   176→- Assert: validate business outcome and state changes
   177→
   178→## Environment-Adaptive Testing
   179→- Local development: in-memory infrastructure for fast feedback (~100ms)
   180→- CI/CD pipeline: production-like infrastructure for integration validation (~2-5s)
   181→- Same scenarios: single source of truth across all environments
   182→

content.text:
<!-- DES-VALIDATION : required -->
<!-- DES-PROJECT-ID : agent-learnings -->
<!-- DES-STEP-ID : 01-02 -->

# DES_METADATA
Step: 01-02
Project: agent-learnings
Command: /nw:execute

# AGENT_IDENTITY
Agent: nw-functional-software-crafter

# SKILL_LOADING
Before starting TDD phases, read your skill files for methodology guidance.
Skills path: ~/.claude/skills/nw/nw-functional-software-crafter/
Always load at PREPARE: tdd-methodology.md, quality-framework.md

# TASK_CONTEXT
Name: Learning CRUD queries, types, and shared contracts
Description: Workspace-scoped CRUD operations, status transitions, listing queries, rate-limit counting. Shared contract types in contracts.ts.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. createLearning persists record with evidence edges and returns created learning
2. updateLearningStatus validates workspace scope before transition; supersedeLearning deactivates old and creates supersedes edge
3. listWorkspaceActiveLearnings and listWorkspacePendingLearnings return workspace-filtered results
4. countRecentSuggestionsByAgent returns count of suggestions within last 7 days
5. LearningSummary, LearningType, LearningStatus, LearningSource types exported from shared/contracts.ts; EntityKind union includes learning

Implementation scope (files to create/modify):
- app/src/server/learning/queries.ts (NEW)
- app/src/server/learning/types.ts (NEW)
- app/src/shared/contracts.ts (MODIFY - add learning types)

Test files:
- tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/milestone-1-schema-and-queries.test.ts (existing, tests already enabled from step 01-01)

Dependencies: 01-01 (complete)
Time estimate: 2h

## Architecture Details

Follow the patterns in observation/queries.ts and suggestion/queries.ts for workspace-scoped CRUD.

### queries.ts Functions:
- `createLearning(surreal, workspace, input)` - Create learning + evidence edges via RELATE
- `updateLearningStatus(surreal, workspace, learningId, newStatus, auditFields)` - Validate workspace scope, transition status
- `supersedeLearning(surreal, workspace, oldLearningId, newLearningId, reason)` - Deactivate old + RELATE new->supersedes->old
- `listWorkspaceActiveLearnings(surreal, workspace, agentType?)` - Active learnings filtered by workspace and optional agent type
- `listWorkspacePendingLearnings(surreal, workspace, limit?)` - Pending approval learnings for feed
- `countRecentSuggestionsByAgent(surreal, workspace, agentType)` - Count suggestions in last 7 days for rate limiting

### types.ts - Internal types:
- `CreateLearningInput` - text, learning_type, priority?, target_agents?, source, suggested_by?, pattern_confidence?, created_by?, evidence_ids?
- Re-export shared contract types

### shared/contracts.ts additions:
```typescript
export const LEARNING_TYPES = ["constraint", "instruction", "precedent"] as const;
export type LearningType = (typeof LEARNING_TYPES)[number];

export const LEARNING_STATUSES = ["active", "pending_approval", "dismissed", "superseded", "deactivated"] as const;
export type LearningStatus = (typeof LEARNING_STATUSES)[number];

export const LEARNING_SOURCES = ["human", "agent"] as const;
export type LearningSource = (typeof LEARNING_SOURCES)[number];

export type LearningSummary = {
  id: string;
  text: string;
  learningType: LearningType;
  status: LearningStatus;
  source: LearningSource;
  priority: EntityPriority;
  targetAgents: string[];
  suggestedBy?: string;
  patternConfidence?: number;
  createdAt: string;
  approvedAt?: string;
  dismissedAt?: string;
  dismissedReason?: string;
  deactivatedAt?: string;
};
```

And add `"learning"` to the EntityKind union type.

### Query Patterns (SurrealDB):
- Use RecordId objects, never raw table:id strings
- Two-step KNN for vector queries (SurrealDB HNSW bug)
- No null - use undefined via optional properties
- RELATE for relation edges (not CREATE)
- For supersession: `RELATE new->supersedes->old SET superseded_at = time::now(), reason = $reason`
- Active learnings query filters: workspace + status="active" + (empty target_agents OR agentType IN target_agents)

### Test expectations (from milestone-1 tests):
The tests create records via createTestLearning() helper (direct SurrealDB) and then verify:
- M1-04: listActiveLearnings returns workspace-filtered results
- M1-05: Status transition from active to deactivated records audit trail
- M1-06: Pending approval learning not returned as active
- M1-07: Superseding marks old as superseded and creates edge
- M1-08: Evidence edges link learning to source entities
- M1-09: Workspace isolation
- M1-11: Priority defaults to medium

Note: The milestone-1 tests use the test kit helpers (createTestLearning, listActiveLearnings, etc.) which query SurrealDB directly. The queries.ts functions you create should follow the same query patterns but be called from route handlers (step 02-02). For THIS step, make sure your queries work correctly by verifying the existing acceptance tests still pass.

## Project Conventions
- Always use `--no-verify -s` when committing
- No null in domain data. Use undefined via optional properties.
- Do NOT create wrapper functions for simple operations. Cast directly with `as`.
- Follow functional paradigm: pure functions where possible, effect boundaries at adapter layer.

# TDD_PHASES
Execute in order:
0. PREPARE - Read existing patterns (observation/queries.ts), understand test expectations
1. RED_ACCEPTANCE - Acceptance tests already exist in milestone-1. Verify current state (they should pass for schema-only tests from 01-01). The queries.ts functions will be validated when used by HTTP routes in step 02-02.
2. RED_UNIT - Write unit tests for any pure logic in queries.ts if applicable. If queries are all DB operations, SKIP.
3. GREEN - Create queries.ts, types.ts, modify contracts.ts. Verify milestone-1 tests still pass.
4. COMMIT - Stage and commit with `--no-verify -s`. Include git trailer: `Step-ID: 01-02`

# QUALITY_GATES
- All milestone-1 tests pass
- Types exported correctly from contracts.ts
- No TypeScript errors in new files (run typecheck if available)

# OUTCOME_RECORDING
After ACTUALLY EXECUTING each phase, record via DES CLI:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/agent-learnings \
      --step-id 01-02 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status EXECUTED \
      --data PASS

For SKIPPED phases:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/agent-learnings \
      --step-id 01-02 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status SKIPPED \
      --data "NOT_APPLICABLE: reason"

# RECORDING_INTEGRITY
- Only record phases you actually executed
- Use DES CLI for all recording — never edit execution-log.yaml manually
- Each phase must be recorded immediately after execution
- Timestamps are generated by the CLI (real UTC), not by you

# BOUNDARY_RULES
- Only create/modify: app/src/server/learning/queries.ts, app/src/server/learning/types.ts, app/src/shared/contracts.ts
- Do not load roadmap.yaml
- Do not modify execution-log.yaml structure (append only via CLI)

# TIMEOUT_INSTRUCTION
Target: 30 turns max. If approaching limit, COMMIT current progress.

TASK BOUNDARY: Implement step 01-02 only. Return control to orchestrator after completion.

1→---
     2→name: tdd-methodology
     3→description: Deep knowledge for Outside-In TDD - double-loop architecture, ATDD integration, port-to-port testing, walking skeletons, and test doubles policy
     4→---
     5→
     6→# Outside-In TDD Methodology
     7→
     8→## Double-Loop TDD Architecture
     9→
    10→Outer loop: ATDD/E2E Tests (customer view) - business requirements, hours-days to green.
    11→Inner loop: Unit Tests (developer view) - technical implementation, minutes to green, RED->GREEN->REFACTOR.
    12→
    13→Outer stays red while inner cycles. Outer drives WHAT to build, inner drives HOW.
    14→Never build components not needed by actual user scenarios.
    15→
    16→## Outside-In vs Inside-Out
    17→
    18→Inside-Out (Classic/bottom-up): discovers collaborators through refactoring. TDD guides design completely.
    19→Outside-In (London/top-down/mockist): knows collaborators upfront, mocks them, implements each moving inward.
    20→
    21→Use Outside-In when: architectural boundaries known (hexagonal), program to interface not implementation.
    22→
    23→## ATDD Integration (Lightweight)
    24→
    25→Original 2008 heavyweight ATDD was "too heavyweight for most real teams." Updated approach (Hendrickson 2024):
    26→- Few Given/When/Then examples, not many | Separate requirements from tests
    27→- Smallest subset of team with relevant skills | Value = shared understanding, not executable specs
    28→- Automate only where high-value
    29→
    30→## BDD Integration
    31→
    32→BDD emerged from Outside-In TDD. Given(context)->When(action)->Then(outcome) maps to outside-in mindset.
    33→BDD reframes TDD as design/specification technique, not just testing. More accessible to stakeholders.
    34→Gherkin: structured format bridging technical/non-technical. Use pragmatically - automate only where high value.
    35→
    36→## Outside-In Development Workflow (Bache)
    37→1. Write Guiding Test (acceptance) from user perspective - thick slice of functionality
    38→2. Start at top-level entry point, design collaborating classes incrementally
    39→3. Use mocks to experiment with interfaces/protocols
    40→4. As each layer implemented, move to previously mocked collaborators, TDD again
    41→5. Never build what isn't needed for actual user scenarios
    42→
    43→## Port-to-Port Testing
    44→
    45→Tests enter through driving port (application service / public API) and assert outcomes at driven port boundaries.
    46→Internal classes (entities, value objects, domain services) exercised indirectly - never instantiated directly in test code.
    47→
    48→Flow: Driving Port -> Application -> Domain -> Driven Port (mocked)
    49→
    50→```python
    51→def test_order_service_processes_payment():
    52→    # Setup - mock driven port (external dependency)
    53→    payment_gateway = MockPaymentGateway()
    54→    order_repo = InMemoryOrderRepository()
    55→
    56→    # Test through driving port (application service)
    57→    order_service = OrderService(payment_gateway, order_repo)
    58→    result = order_service.place_order(customer_id, items)
    59→
    60→    # Assert observable outcomes
    61→    assert result.is_confirmed()
    62→    payment_gateway.verify_charge_called(amount=100.00)
    63→```
    64→
    65→## Unit of Behavior (not Unit of Code)
    66→
    67→Test = story about the problem your code solves. Granularity related to stakeholder needs.
    68→A unit of behavior may span multiple classes. Test from driving port to driven port boundary.
    69→Key question: "Can you explain this test to a stakeholder?" If not, you're testing implementation details.
    70→
    71→## Classical vs Mockist Verification
    72→
    73→Classical TDD: real objects | state verification | less coupled to implementation | survives refactoring better.
    74→Mockist TDD: mocks for objects with behavior | behavior verification | lighter setup | more coupled to impl.
    75→Best practice: combine strategically. Behavior verification at layer boundaries, state verification within layers.
    76→
    77→## Test Doubles Taxonomy (Meszaros)
    78→- Dummy: passed but never used
    79→- Fake: working impl with shortcuts (in-memory DB)
    80→- Stub: predefined answers
    81→- Spy: stub that records interactions
    82→- Mock: pre-programmed with expectations for behavior verification
    83→
    84→Choose type by need: mock for interaction design | stub when don't care about interaction | fake for integration bridge.
    85→
    86→## Hexagonal Architecture Testing Strategy
    87→
    88→### Domain Layer
    89→Tested indirectly through driving port (application service) unit tests with real domain objects.
    90→Domain entities, value objects, domain services are implementation details. Testing them directly couples tests to internal structure.
    91→
    92→Exception: Standalone domain logic with complex algorithms (e.g., pricing engine) MAY be tested directly when complexity warrants it and the class has a stable public interface. This is the EXCEPTION, not the rule.
    93→
    94→### Application Layer
    95→Classical TDD within layer, Mockist TDD at port boundaries.
    96→Use real Order, Money, Customer objects in application service tests.
    97→Mock IPaymentGateway, IEmailService ports when testing orchestration.
    98→
    99→### Infrastructure Layer
   100→Integration tests ONLY - no unit tests for adapters.
   101→Mocking infrastructure inside an adapter test is testing the mock, not the adapter.
   102→Use real infrastructure (testcontainers, in-memory databases) to verify actual behavior.
   103→
   104→### E2E Tests
   105→Minimal mocking - only truly external systems (3rd party APIs beyond your control).
   106→Use real domain services, application services, repositories.
   107→
   108→## Test Doubles Policy
   109→
   110→Acceptable (port boundaries only):
   111→- `Mock<IPaymentGateway>` - external payment service port
   112→- `Mock<IEmailService>` - external email provider port
   113→- `InMemoryUserRepository` - fake for fast tests (implements IUserRepository port)
   114→
   115→Do not mock inside the hexagon:
   116→- Domain entities (Order, Customer) - use real objects
   117→- Value objects (Money, Email) - cheap to create, deterministic
   118→- Application services (OrderProcessor) - use real with mocked ports
   119→- Domain services (PricingService) - use real objects
   120→
   121→## Walking Skeleton Protocol
   122→
   123→At most one walking skeleton per new feature. When `is_walking_skeleton: true` in roadmap:
   124→- Write exactly ONE E2E/acceptance test proving end-to-end wiring
   125→- Implement thinnest possible slice - hardcoded values, minimal branching
   126→- Do NOT write unit tests - the E2E test IS the deliverable
   127→- Do NOT add error handling, edge cases, or validation
   128→- Skip inner TDD loop, go directly from RED_ACCEPTANCE to GREEN
   129→
   130→E2E tests are inherently slow and flaky. The walking skeleton is the ONE justified E2E test per feature.
   131→
   132→## E2E Test Management
   133→
   134→Enable ONE E2E test at a time to prevent commit blocks:
   135→1. All E2E tests except first one marked with skip/ignore
   136→2. Complete first scenario through Outside-In TDD
   137→3. Commit working implementation
   138→4. Enable next E2E test
   139→5. Repeat until all scenarios implemented
   140→
   141→## Step Method Pattern
   142→
   143→Step methods call production services, not test infrastructure:
   144→
   145→```csharp
   146→[When("business action occurs")]
   147→public async Task WhenBusinessActionOccurs()
   148→{
   149→    var service = _serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<IBusinessService>();
   150→    _result = await service.PerformBusinessActionAsync(_testData);
   151→}
   152→```
   153→
   154→Scaffold unimplemented collaborators with `NotImplementedException`:
   155→```csharp
   156→throw new NotImplementedException(
   157→    "Business capability not yet implemented - driven by outside-in TDD"
   158→);
   159→```
   160→
   161→## Business-Focused Testing
   162→
   163→### Unit Test Naming
   164→- Class pattern: `<DrivingPort>Should`
   165→- Method pattern: `<ExpectedOutcome>_When<SpecificBehavior>[_Given<Preconditions>]`
   166→- Example: `AccountServiceShould.IncreaseBalance_WhenDepositMade_GivenSufficientFunds`
   167→
   168→### Behavior Types
   169→- Command behavior: changes system state (Given-When-Then)
   170→- Query behavior: returns state projection (Given-Then)
   171→- Process behavior: orchestrates multiple commands/queries
   172→
   173→### Test Structure
   174→- Arrange: set up business context and test data
   175→- Act: perform business action
   176→- Assert: validate business outcome and state changes
   177→
   178→## Environment-Adaptive Testing
   179→- Local development: in-memory infrastructure for fast feedback (~100ms)
   180→- CI/CD pipeline: production-like infrastructure for integration validation (~2-5s)
   181→- Same scenarios: single source of truth across all environments
   182→

data.command:
Review this conversation for items that were discussed but NOT logged via the Brain MCP tools.

IMPORTANT: If this was a trivial session (greetings, test messages, simple Q&A with no implementation work), immediately respond with:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"No substantive work to log"}
Do NOT explain why there is nothing to log.

Otherwise, check each category:

1. **Decisions**: Architecture or design choices where alternatives existed (e.g. choosing a pattern, library, data model, or trade-off). Do NOT log mechanical bugfixes, typo corrections, or changes with only one correct approach. Call create_provisional_decision for each genuine decision.
2. **Questions**: Unresolved questions raised but not logged. Call ask_question for each.
3. **Observations**: Contradictions, duplication, missing items, deprecated patterns, or anomalies noticed. Call log_observation for each.
4. **Task progress**: Tasks worked on whose status wasn't updated. Call update_task_status for each.
5. **Subtasks identified**: Work items or follow-up tasks discussed but not created. Call create_subtask for each (requires a parent task).
6. **Suggestions**: Optimizations, risks, opportunities, missing elements, or potential pivots noticed during work. Call create_suggestion for each with category (optimization|risk|opportunity|conflict|missing|pivot), rationale, and confidence (0-1).

After logging everything, respond with valid JSON in this exact shape:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"<one-line session summary>","decisions_made":["<decision-id>"],"questions_asked":["<question-id>"],"tasks_progressed":[{"task_id":"<task-id>","from_status":"<from>","to_status":"<to>"}],"files_changed":[{"path":"<path>","change_type":"created|modified|deleted"}],"observations_logged":["<observation-id>"],"subtasks_created":["<subtask-id>"],"suggestions_created":["<suggestion-id>"]}

If you cannot log required items (for example MCP unavailable), respond with:
{"decision":"block","reason":"Log these items first: ..."}

hookInfos.promptText:
Review this conversation for items that were discussed but NOT logged via the Brain MCP tools.

IMPORTANT: If this was a trivial session (greetings, test messages, simple Q&A with no implementation work), immediately respond with:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"No substantive work to log"}
Do NOT explain why there is nothing to log.

Otherwise, check each category:

1. **Decisions**: Architecture or design choices where alternatives existed (e.g. choosing a pattern, library, data model, or trade-off). Do NOT log mechanical bugfixes, typo corrections, or changes with only one correct approach. Call create_provisional_decision for each genuine decision.
2. **Questions**: Unresolved questions raised but not logged. Call ask_question for each.
3. **Observations**: Contradictions, duplication, missing items, deprecated patterns, or anomalies noticed. Call log_observation for each.
4. **Task progress**: Tasks worked on whose status wasn't updated. Call update_task_status for each.
5. **Subtasks identified**: Work items or follow-up tasks discussed but not created. Call create_subtask for each (requires a parent task).
6. **Suggestions**: Optimizations, risks, opportunities, missing elements, or potential pivots noticed during work. Call create_suggestion for each with category (optimization|risk|opportunity|conflict|missing|pivot), rationale, and confidence (0-1).

After logging everything, respond with valid JSON in this exact shape:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"<one-line session summary>","decisions_made":["<decision-id>"],"questions_asked":["<question-id>"],"tasks_progressed":[{"task_id":"<task-id>","from_status":"<from>","to_status":"<to>"}],"files_changed":[{"path":"<path>","change_type":"created|modified|deleted"}],"observations_logged":["<observation-id>"],"subtasks_created":["<subtask-id>"],"suggestions_created":["<suggestion-id>"]}

If you cannot log required items (for example MCP unavailable), respond with:
{"decision":"block","reason":"Log these items first: ..."}

input.prompt:
<!-- DES-VALIDATION : required -->
<!-- DES-PROJECT-ID : agent-learnings -->
<!-- DES-STEP-ID : 01-03 -->

# DES_METADATA
Step: 01-03
Project: agent-learnings
Command: /nw:execute

# AGENT_IDENTITY
Agent: nw-functional-software-crafter

# SKILL_LOADING
Before starting TDD phases, read your skill files for methodology guidance.
Skills path: ~/.claude/skills/nw/nw-functional-software-crafter/
Always load at PREPARE: tdd-methodology.md, quality-framework.md

# TASK_CONTEXT
Name: JIT learning loader and prompt formatter
Description: Load active learnings with priority sort and token budget (~500). Pure formatter groups output by type as Workspace Learnings section.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. Constraints always included even when they alone exceed 500-token budget; observation logged but no constraints dropped
2. Instructions fill remaining budget in priority order; oversized instructions skipped, next tried
3. Precedents included only when contextEmbedding provided and similarity > 0.70; excluded silently when no contextEmbedding
4. Priority sort: human > agent source, high > medium > low priority, newest first
5. Formatter outputs grouped sections (Constraints, Instructions, Precedents) under Workspace Learnings heading

Implementation scope:
- app/src/server/learning/loader.ts (NEW)
- app/src/server/learning/formatter.ts (NEW)

Test files:
- tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/milestone-2-jit-loader-and-formatter.test.ts (existing, has .skip tests - ENABLE them)

Dependencies: 01-02 (complete)
Time estimate: 2h

## Detailed Design

### loader.ts - `loadActiveLearnings(surreal, workspace, agentType, contextEmbedding?)`

1. Query constraints + instructions (non-semantic) from learning table:
```sql
SELECT id, text, learning_type, source, priority, target_agents, created_at
FROM learning
WHERE workspace = $workspace
  AND status = "active"
  AND learning_type IN ["constraint", "instruction"]
  AND (array::len(target_agents) = 0 OR $agentType IN target_agents)
ORDER BY
  source = "human" DESC,
  priority = "high" DESC,
  priority = "medium" DESC,
  created_at DESC
LIMIT 50;
```

2. If contextEmbedding provided, query precedents (semantic - two-step KNN per SurrealDB HNSW bug):
```sql
-- Step 1: KNN candidates
LET $candidates = SELECT id, text, learning_type, source, priority, target_agents, created_at, workspace,
    vector::similarity::cosine(embedding, $contextEmbedding) AS similarity
  FROM learning
  WHERE embedding <|20, COSINE|> $contextEmbedding;

-- Step 2: Filter
SELECT * FROM $candidates
  WHERE workspace = $workspace
  AND status = "active"
  AND learning_type = "precedent"
  AND (array::len(target_agents) = 0 OR $agentType IN target_agents)
  AND similarity > 0.70
  ORDER BY similarity DESC
  LIMIT 10;
```

3. Apply token budget (~500 tokens):
   - Token estimation: `estimateTokens(text) = Math.ceil(text.trim().split(/\s+/).length / 0.75)`
   - Constraints: ALWAYS included, never dropped. If constraints alone exceed budget, include all and log warning (return a flag or log observation)
   - Instructions: fill remaining budget in priority order. If one is too large, skip it and try the next.
   - Precedents: fill remaining budget by similarity order.

4. Return sorted learnings with their types for the formatter.

### formatter.ts - `formatLearningsSection(learnings)` (PURE FUNCTION)

Takes array of learnings, groups by type, renders as markdown:
```
## Workspace Learnings

### Constraints
- Never use null for domain data values

### Instructions  
- Use RecordId objects, never raw table:id strings

### Precedents
- PostgreSQL chosen over MySQL for JSON support (similarity: 0.85)
```

Only include sections that have learnings. If no learnings, return empty string.

## Project Conventions
- Always use `--no-verify -s` when committing
- No null - use undefined via optional properties
- Functional paradigm: formatter.ts is pure, loader.ts has IO at boundary only
- Two-step KNN pattern required for vector queries (SurrealDB HNSW+WHERE bug)
- Read existing code: app/src/server/learning/queries.ts (created in 01-02) for patterns

## Test Details (milestone-2)
The tests create learnings directly in SurrealDB with specific priorities, types, sources, and target_agents, then call loader/formatter functions and verify:
- M2-01: human before agent sort
- M2-02: high before medium/low
- M2-03: constraints never dropped even over budget
- M2-04: instructions fill remaining budget
- M2-05: empty target_agents = all agents
- M2-06: specific target_agents filtering
- M2-07: empty workspace = empty list
- M2-08: deactivated excluded
- M2-09: superseded excluded

READ the test file to understand exact expectations before implementing. Remove .skip from tests as you implement.

# TDD_PHASES
Execute in order:
0. PREPARE - Read milestone-2 test file, understand expectations, read queries.ts
1. RED_ACCEPTANCE - Enable milestone-2 tests, verify they fail
2. RED_UNIT - Write unit tests for formatter.ts (pure function) and estimateTokens
3. GREEN - Implement loader.ts and formatter.ts. Pass all tests.
4. COMMIT - Stage and commit with `--no-verify -s`. Trailer: `Step-ID: 01-03`

# QUALITY_GATES
- All milestone-1 + milestone-2 tests pass
- formatter.ts has no IO imports (pure function)
- Token budget logic works correctly

# OUTCOME_RECORDING
After ACTUALLY EXECUTING each phase, record via DES CLI:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/agent-learnings \
      --step-id 01-03 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status EXECUTED \
      --data PASS

For SKIPPED phases:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/agent-learnings \
      --step-id 01-03 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status SKIPPED \
      --data "NOT_APPLICABLE: reason"

# RECORDING_INTEGRITY
- Only record phases you actually executed
- Use DES CLI for all recording — never edit execution-log.yaml manually
- Each phase must be recorded immediately after execution
- Timestamps are generated by the CLI (real UTC), not by you

# BOUNDARY_RULES
- Only create/modify: app/src/server/learning/loader.ts, app/src/server/learning/formatter.ts
- You may also create tests/unit/learning-formatter.test.ts for unit tests
- Do not load roadmap.yaml

# TIMEOUT_INSTRUCTION
Target: 30 turns max. If approaching limit, COMMIT current progress.

TASK BOUNDARY: Implement step 01-03 only. Return control to orchestrator after completion.

content.thinking:
Phase 1 complete (01-01, 01-02, 01-03). Now for Phase 2, the dependency graph allows:
- 02-01 (prompt injection) depends on 01-03 ✓
- 02-02 (HTTP endpoints) depends on 01-03 ✓
- 03-01 (pattern detection) depends on 01-02 ✓
- 03-02 (collision detection) depends on 01-03 ✓

So 02-01, 02-02, 03-01, and 03-02 could theoretically all run now. But I should be careful about parallel execution - they modify different files so they shouldn't conflict. Let me run 02-01 and 03-01 in parallel since they touch completely different files. Then 02-02 and 03-02.


Actually, wait - I need to reconsider the file dependencies more carefully. 02-02 modifies the server startup and feed routes, while 03-02 creates the collision detection logic that 02-02 actually needs for its approval flow. So I can't run them truly in parallel - 03-02 needs to complete first so that 02-02 can integrate the collision check into the activation flow. The acceptance tests show that collision detection is tested later in milestone-6, so the approve action in 02-02 should just handle the status transition and audit trail, with collision checking added when 03-02 creates the collision.ts module. I'll run 02-01 and 03-01 in parallel since they're independent, then tackle 02-02 and 03-02 sequentially.

content.text: Phase 1 complete (3/3 steps). Now executing Phase 2 steps. Steps 02-01 and 03-01 have no shared files — running them in parallel.

<!-- DES-VALIDATION : required -->

# DES_METADATA
Step: 02-01
Project: agent-learnings
Command: /nw:execute

# AGENT_IDENTITY
Agent: nw-functional-software-crafter

# SKILL_LOADING
Skills path: ~/.claude/skills/nw/nw-functional-software-crafter/
Always load at PREPARE: tdd-methodology.md, quality-framework.md

# TASK_CONTEXT
Name: Inject learnings into all four prompt builders
Description: Additive integration of loadActiveLearnings + formatLearningsSection into chat agent, PM agent, observer agent, and MCP context builder.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. Chat agent system prompt includes Workspace Learnings section using project description as contextEmbedding
2. PM agent system prompt includes Workspace Learnings section using project description as contextEmbedding
3. Observer agent system prompt includes Workspace Learnings with constraints and instructions only (no contextEmbedding, no precedents)
4. MCP context packets include learnings array using task description as contextEmbedding
5. Learnings section positioned before conversation/entity context in all prompt builders

Implementation scope:
- app/src/server/chat/context.ts (MODIFY)
- app/src/server/agents/pm/prompt.ts (MODIFY)
- app/src/server/agents/observer/prompt.ts (MODIFY)
- app/src/server/mcp/context-builder.ts (MODIFY)

Test files:
- tests/acceptance/agent-learnings/milestone-3-prompt-injection.test.ts (existing, has .skip tests - ENABLE them)

Dependencies: 01-03 (complete)

## Integration Pattern

All four prompt builders follow the same pattern:
```typescript
import { loadActiveLearnings } from "../learning/loader";
import { formatLearningsSection } from "../learning/formatter";

// Inside the build function:
const learnings = await loadActiveLearnings(surreal, workspaceRecord, agentType, contextEmbedding);
const learningsSection = formatLearningsSection(learnings);
// Append learningsSection to system prompt sections BEFORE conversation/entity context
```

Context embedding sources per builder:
- **Chat agent** (`chat/context.ts`): embedding of conversation's project description (if project linked)
- **PM agent** (`agents/pm/prompt.ts`): embedding of project description
- **Observer** (`agents/observer/prompt.ts`): NO contextEmbedding (gets constraints + instructions only, never precedents)
- **MCP** (`mcp/context-builder.ts`): embedding of task description from context packet request

If contextEmbedding is unavailable (no project linked, no task description), precedents are excluded silently — not an error.

## Important Notes
- These are ADDITIVE changes — do not modify existing function signatures
- Read each file first to understand current structure before adding learnings integration
- The learnings section should be positioned BEFORE conversation/entity context in the prompt
- Read milestone-3 test file to understand exact expectations
- The tests check that learnings appear in prompts/context and that agent-type filtering works
- Observer specifically should NOT get precedents (it has no contextEmbedding)

## Project Conventions
- Always use `--no-verify -s` when committing
- No null - use undefined via optional properties
- Functional paradigm

# TDD_PHASES
Execute in order:
0. PREPARE - Read all 4 prompt builder files and milestone-3 tests
1. RED_ACCEPTANCE - Enable milestone-3 tests, verify they fail
2. RED_UNIT - SKIP (NOT_APPLICABLE: integration changes only, no new pure logic)
3. GREEN - Add learnings integration to all 4 builders. Pass all tests.
4. COMMIT - `--no-verify -s`. Trailer: `Step-ID: 02-01`

# QUALITY_GATES
- All milestone-1 + milestone-2 + milestone-3 tests pass
- No changes to existing function signatures
- TypeScript typecheck clean

# OUTCOME_RECORDING
After ACTUALLY EXECUTING each phase, record via DES CLI:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/agent-learnings \
      --step-id 02-01 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status EXECUTED \
      --data PASS

For SKIPPED phases:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/agent-learnings \
      --step-id 02-01 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status SKIPPED \
      --data "NOT_APPLICABLE: reason"

# RECORDING_INTEGRITY
- Only record phases you actually executed
- Use DES CLI for all recording — never edit execution-log.yaml manually
- Each phase must be recorded immediately after execution

# BOUNDARY_RULES
- Only modify: chat/context.ts, agents/pm/prompt.ts, agents/observer/prompt.ts, mcp/context-builder.ts
- Do not load roadmap.yaml

# TIMEOUT_INSTRUCTION
Target: 30 turns max. COMMIT current progress if approaching limit.

TASK BOUNDARY: Implement step 02-01 only. Return control to orchestrator after completion.

- Read each file first to understand current structure before adding learnings integration
- The learnings section should be positioned BEFORE conversation/entity context in the prompt
- Read milestone-3 test file to understand exact expectations
- The tests check that learnings appear in prompts/context and that agent-type filtering works
- Observer specifically should NOT get precedents (it has no contextEmbedding)

## Project Conventions
- Always use `--no-verify -s` when committing
- No null - use undefined via optional properties
- Functional paradigm

# TDD_PHASES
Execute in order:
0. PREPARE - Read all 4 prompt builder files and milestone-3 tests
1. RED_ACCEPTANCE - Enable milestone-3 tests, verify they fail
2. RED_UNIT - SKIP (NOT_APPLICABLE: integration changes only, no new pure logic)
3. GREEN - Add learnings integration to all 4 builders. Pass all tests.
4. COMMIT - `--no-verify -s`. Trailer: `Step-ID: 02-01`

# QUALITY_GATES
- All milestone-1 + milestone-2 + milestone-3 tests pass
- No changes to existing function signatures
- TypeScript typecheck clean

# OUTCOME_RECORDING
After ACTUALLY EXECUTING each phase, record via DES CLI:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/agent-learnings \
      --step-id 02-01 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status EXECUTED \
      --data PASS

For SKIPPED phases:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/agent-learnings \
      --step-id 02-01 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status SKIPPED \
      --data "NOT_APPLICABLE: reason"

# RECORDING_INTEGRITY
- Only record phases you actually executed
- Use DES CLI for all recording — never edit execution-log.yaml manually
- Each phase must be recorded immediately after execution

# BOUNDARY_RULES
- Only modify: chat/context.ts, agents/pm/prompt.ts, agents/observer/prompt.ts, mcp/context-builder.ts
- Do not load roadmap.yaml

# TIMEOUT_INSTRUCTION
Target: 30 turns max. COMMIT current progress if approaching limit.
